I rollen som projektledare för telekommunikation ansvarar du dels för att IT-lösningar för telekomdelen kommer på plats till våra nya anläggningar i nätet och dels att fiberdisponering planeras och utförs för respektive inkommande ledning.
   

  

 Du kommer i huvudsak att agera som delprojektledare i Svenska Kraftnäts stora anläggningsprojekt/revisionsprojekt gällande utbyggnad och förnyelse av transmissionsnätet för el. I projekten har du helhetsansvaret för IT-leveransen, som består av hårdvara för tele- och datakommunikation och optoanläggningar i samband med stationsbyggnation/stationsförnyelse. Rollen är omväxlande och kräver att du arbetar operativt här och nu samtidigt som du är taktisk och planerar för teknik som ska byggas om flera år.
   

  

 I arbetsuppgifterna ingår bland annat planering av de projektaktiviteter som ska utföras inom ditt ansvarsområde, vägledning av entreprenörer, koordinering av teknisk personal och besiktning av utfört arbete med mera. Tjänsten kommer bland annat att innefatta att:
   

  

* Delta och sammanställa kravspecifikation som omfattar telekommunikationsutrustningen till den tekniska beskrivningen inför upphandling av entreprenader för nya stationsbyggnationer/stationsförnyelser.
* Delta i projekteringen för den fiberoptiska delen inför upphandling av entreprenader för ledningsåtgärder. Framtagen lösning ska vara komplett och genomtänkt för att kunna upprätthålla kommunikationen optimalt i samband med genomförandet av projektet.
* Delta i anläggningsprojekt i genomförandefasen där du ansvarar för att en komplett IT leverans med tillhörande telekommunikationslösning och fiberoptisk lösning blir installerad enligt tidplan samt ta fram tekniska handlingar.
* Koordinera arbetet i projekten mellan den egna enheten och övriga teknikområden.
* Löpande avstämning och koordinering för den fiberoptiska delen i projektet för vidare inkoppling av telekommunikationslösningen.
* Granska all löpande dokumentation från projekten och säkerställa så att slutdokumentationen blir godkänd för en anläggning (projekt) och blir uppdaterad i berörda system.
* Arbeta med att förbättra och utveckla arbetssätt, tekniska riktlinjer och rutiner inom enhetens ansvarsområden.


 Rollen tillhör enheten för Fiber och anläggningsprojekt som är en del av Svenska kraftnäts organisation IT för Nät och anläggningar. Enheten består av ett tjugotal medarbetare som ansvarar för planering, drift, förvaltning och utveckling av Svenska kraftnäts samhällsviktiga fiber- och kommunikationsnät.
   

  

**Om dig**
**Skallkrav:**
 Rollen är väldigt självständig och ställer därmed stora krav på ditt personliga ledarskap i form av egna initiativ, tydlighet och ansvarskänsla. I rollen agerar du alltid som en representant för hela vår verksamhet och du kommer att ha många kontakter inom och utom organisationen, varför vi ser att du är en person med en naturlig fallenhet för att skapa goda relationer och bra samarbeten i de olika konstellationer och grupper du ingår i. Du driver flertalet projekt samtidigt och behöver även vara strukturerad och trygg i dig själv.
   

  

 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har en högskoleutbildning inom telekom/IT eller likvärdig utbildning eller motsvarande kompetens förvärvad på annat sätt som Svenska kraftnät bedömer som likvärdig. Vidare har du:
   

  

* Flerårig aktuell erfarenhet av att driva tekniskt komplexa projekt inom IT eller telekom.
* Flerårig aktuell och relevant erfarenhet av arbete med telekom/IT-miljöer, så som WAN, telekomnät (DTN/DWDM), telefoni.
* B-körkort
* God förmåga att uttrycka dig i tal och skrift på svenska och engelska.


 Vi ser det som
 **meriterande** 
 om du har:
   

  

* Arbetat med projekt i entreprenadform.
* Erfarenhet av praktiskt arbete med installation och drift av IT-miljöer för Telekom.
* Dokumenterad projektledarutbildning.
* Arbetat som projekt-/byggledare för optoanläggningar eller motsvarande.


**Bra att veta**
* Tjänsten är placerad i Stockholm/Sundbyberg
* Möjlighet till distansarbete upp till 50 %
* Resor förekommer regelbundet i denna tjänst, ungefär 3 - 4 dagar i månaden
* Sista ansökningsdag är 21 maj, vi använder urvalsfrågor istället för personligt brev.
* Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning
* Förmåner | Svenska kraftnät


 Vid frågor om tjänsten kontakta gärna rekryterande chef rekryterande chef Victor Skogsman, tel. nr. 010-475 81 85. Vid frågor om rekryteringsprocessen kontakta ansvarig rekryterare Maria Sahlén, 072-459 85 27 (maria.sahlen2@svk.se). Fackliga representanter är Annika Ingeborn, SACO, 010 475 87 72 och Erica Midfjäll ST, 010 475 87 31. Du når oss också via e-post: fornamn.efternamn@svk.se
   

  

 Jobba här – bli en viktig del av vår energiframtid | Svenska kraftnät
   

  

 Vi undanber oss direktkontakt med bemannings- och rekryteringsföretag samt säljare i samband med rekrytering, vi har upphandlade avtal som gäller för Svenska kraftnät som statlig myndighet.
   

  

 Övrig information
   

  

 Vi ställer höga krav på våra medarbetares säkerhets- och sekretessmedvetenhet. Innan ett beslut om anställning fattas kommer en säkerhetsprövning enligt säkerhetsskyddslagen (2018:585) att genomföras i de fall befattningen innebär hantering av säkerhetsskyddsklassificerade uppgifter eller deltagande i säkerhetskänslig verksamhet. Om anställningen avser befattning i säkerhetsklass kommer säkerhetsprövningen även att innefatta en registerkontroll och, i vissa fall, en särskild personutredning. För anställning på Svenska kraftnät kan det vara ett krav på svenskt medborgarskap i vissa befattningar. Med anställningen följer en skyldighet att krigsplaceras. För mer information om säkerhetsprövning Svenska kraftnät arbetar för att främja mångfald och motverka diskriminering och ser gärna sökanden med olika bakgrund och erfarenheter till våra rekryteringar. Lär känna Svenska kraftnät och hur det är att arbeta hos oss: Lär känna oss på Svenska kraftnät

title: Senior Software Engineer.
description:
**ABOUT SINCH**
 Sinch is pioneering the way the world communicates. More than 150,000 businesses - including Google, Uber, Paypal, Visa, Tinder, and many others - rely on Sinch's Customer Communications Cloud to power engaging customer experiences through mobile messaging, voice, and email. Whether you need to verify users or craft omnichannel campaigns, Sinch makes it easy. Our AI-infused Super Network, APIs, and applications ensure you can connect with your customers reliably and securely, at every step of their journey. At Sinch we "Dream Big", "Win Together", "Keep it simple", and "Make it Happen". These values are our foundation!
   

  

**DESCRIPTION**
 We are looking for a strong Software Engineer to join our product engineering team in Stockholm (remote within Sweden possible). This role is a 50/50 split between backend and frontend development, with a strong focus on Java backend services and modern React-based web applications. You will work in a small cross-functional team owning the full product lifecycle - from design and implementation to deployment and monitoring. We believe in "you build it, you run it."
   

 Tech Stack: Java, NodeJS, React, Typescript
   

  

* Design, develop, and maintain backend services in Java
* Build modern, scalable frontend applications using React and TypeScript
* Contribute to architectural decisions across backend and frontend
* Improve performance, scalability, and reliability of core systems
* Collaborate closely with product, design, and other engineering teams
* Participate in DevOps practices (CI/CD, monitoring, cloud deployments)


  

**REQUIREMENTS**
  

* Strong experience building backend systems in Java
* Experience building REST APIs and distributed systems
* Solid experience with React and TypeScript
* Good understanding of frontend architecture and modern web standards
* Experience working in cloud-native environments (AWS, Kubernetes is a plus)
* Experience making high-impact architectural decisions
* Fluent in English


 Our corporate language is English, please submit your application in English.
   

  

 OUR HIRING PROCESS
   

 We are committed to ensuring a recruitment process that is fair, objective, consistent, and inclusive. Our approach includes structured, competency-based interviews designed to evaluate your skills, experience, and qualifications relevant to the role. At times, we may include a data-driven assessment to enhance our hiring success and identify candidates likely to excel. We believe in a two-way process and encourage you to ask questions throughout the journey. If this role isn't what you're looking for, please explore the other opportunities listed on our career page: https://www.sinch.com/careers/. No matter who you are, we hope you find an exciting path forward - hopefully with us!

title: Head of AI Innovation & Digital Solutions Quant
description:
**We are looking for a visionary yet hands-on leader — our own "Q" — to shape and drive Quant's digital and AI future.** 




 In this role, you will combine strategy, innovation, and execution, turning ideas into scalable solutions that create real business impact. You will also play a key leadership role in inspiring, guiding, and enabling the organization to adopt new ways of working through AI and digital solutions. You will work closely with Business Development and Finance to ensure that digital and AI initiatives deliver measurable value.
 



 Quant is a global leader in industrial maintenance services, supporting customers across multiple industries worldwide. With over 30 years of experience, we improve safety, productivity, and asset performance at more than 400 facilities globally.
 



 Headquartered in Stockholm, Quant operates across approximately 50 sites worldwide and employs around 1,700 people.
 



**This is not a traditional digital role — it is about redefining how an industrial services company creates value through modern technology** 
 .
 



**Key Responsibilities** 



* Define and drive Quant's AI and digital strategy
* Identify and implement high-impact, scalable use cases
* Lead the development of AI-driven services and solutions
* Drive adoption of digital tools across operations and commercial teams
* Build and leverage partnerships with startups, tech partners, and academia
* Contribute hands-on to solution design, prototyping, and rollout
* Ensure clear financial impact and ROI in collaboration with Finance



**Your Background** 



* 5–10 years in digital, AI, analytics, or tech-driven roles
* Proven track record of delivering business impact through technology
* Strong knowledge of AI, data platforms, cloud, and automation
* Experience combining strategy with hands-on execution
* Experience as a leader, with the ability to build, engage, and develop teams



**Your Profile** 



* A leader who can inspire, influence, and drive changes
* Strategic and execution-focused
* Experience leading cross-functional initiatives and driving change
* Business-oriented mindset and strong collaboration skills
* Creative, curious, and solution-oriented
* Strong business acumen with the ability to translate technology into value
* Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills



**What Success Looks Like** 



* AI and digital solutions widely adopted across the business
* New digital services driving growth and differentiation
* More efficient, data-driven operations with clear financial impact



**Location:** 
 Stockholm (HQ)
 



  
 



**Reports to:** 
 Head of Business Development | Matrix to Group CFO
 



**Why Join Us?** 




 We offer an exciting opportunity in a global organization, where you can make a real impact while continuously developing both our processes and your own expertise.
 



 As an employee at Quant, you will have access to a range of benefits, including wellness allowances, negotiated benefits, and insurance solutions. We also offer flexibility with the opportunity to split your time between remote work and the office.
 



 Interviews will be conducted on a rolling basis, so we encourage you to apply as soon as possible, but no later than June 16, 2026. We look forward to welcoming our new colleague.

title: Sales Manager / Future Country Manager
description:
**Do you have what it takes to launch a Premium Beverage Startup in Stockholm?**
 📍
 **Stockholm, Sweden | Full-Time / Freelance | Monthly Retainer + Uncapped Commission**
**🎯 The Role**
 We don’t believe in slow starts. After a successful run in Belgium, Gullis Drinks is hitting the streets of Stockholm, and we’re looking for a "vinnarskalle" (a winner’s mentality) to lead the charge. You'll own the Stockholm market, proving that natural, premium juice belongs in every high-end deli and trend-setting convenience store from Vasastan to Södermalm.
   

  

 We firmly believe that high-growth sales require more than just a pitch, it requires a deep understanding of local retail culture and the grit to secure the best shelf space. Since you are our first Swedish hire, you will be the face of the brand and have total ownership over our local expansion strategy.
   

  

**💻 What You'll Do**
* Own the Streets: Dominate the inner city by visiting 10–15 hand-picked independent retailers and cafés daily.
* Win the "Fridge-Front": Capture market share by securing eye-level, front-of-fridge placements for our premium bottles.
* Architect the Environment: Onboard every new account into our Swedish Stockfiller system, ensuring data-driven re-orders flow seamlessly from day one.
* Master Brand Aesthetics: Ensure Gullis looks as good as it tastes by placing POS materials with precision and style.
* The 90-Day Sprint: Execute a "Blitzscale" phase with a clear target of 50+ premium listings in your first three months.


**🚀 The Mission**
 Our vision is to reclaim "cool" for natural, sugar-free drinks. We are turning a childhood heritage into Stockholm’s most desired premium brand. Nature provided the vitamins; we provided the brand; you provide the growth.
   

  

**✨ About You**
 You are a "Stockholm Closer" with a passion for premium products and urban retail culture. You thrive on the adrenaline of a sales sprint and view a "nej" as merely the start of a negotiation. While your sales craft is exceptional, you also have the stylistic eye to ensure our brand pops in every store you touch. You are likely an early adopter of new trends and have the independence to hit the ground running without hand-holding.
   

  

**✅ Requirements**
* 🇸🇪 Local Cultural Fluency: You speak the language of Stockholm retail and understand the "fika" business culture.
* ⚡ The Hunter Mindset: Proven track record of high-velocity prospecting and closing deals in fast-paced environments.
* 📦 Strategic Thinking: Experience building or managing retail accounts and ensuring long-term shelf dominance.
* 📂 Grit & Style: A portfolio or track record that demonstrates both the "hustle" to scale and the ability to represent a premium aesthetic.
* 🎓 Independence: You are ready to work independently as "Employee #1" in Sweden.


**➕ Bonus Points**
* Existing relationships with premium delis, bars, or major players like ICA and Coop.
* Experience launching international brands into the Swedish market.
* A background in high-end food & beverage sales.
* Swedish language proficiency


 🌟
 **Why Join Gullis Drinks?**
* 💰 High-Stakes Reward: A solid monthly retainer plus an aggressive, uncapped commission for every listing and crate sold.
* 🚀 Ground Floor Opportunity: The chance to lead our Swedish expansion and transition into a permanent leadership role.
* 🍹 Product Pride: Represent a brand based on 100% natural juice with no added sugars or synthetic additives.
* 🤝 Direct Impact: Work side-by-side with the founder to make Stockholm "real" again.


 👉
 **Ready to Make an Impact?** 
 If you have the grit to scale a startup and the style to represent a premium brand, we’d love to hear from you.

title: Financial Analyst
description:
**About Areim** 




 Areim is an independent Nordic fund manager, connecting capital, real assets, and expertise. Headquartered in Stockholm, with offices in Copenhagen, Helsinki, Oslo, and London, Areim has invested in real assets since 2003, across sectors including light industrial, data centre, office, logistics, and residential. The firm employs over 100 people and manages assets totalling EUR 5 billion.
 



  





 As the company continues to grow, we are now looking for a Financial Analyst who wants to be part of contributing to financial follow-up, analysis, and development in an entrepreneurial environment with a fast pace and great variety. Here you will have the opportunity to work closely with senior decision-makers and grow together with a business in continuous development.
 



  





**About the Role** 




 As a Financial Analyst at Areim, you will be part of a growing finance function focused on financial monitoring, analysis, and reporting for the Areim group. The role offers broad exposure to complex financial structures and involves close collaboration with the CFO, Finance Director, and other senior functions within the organisation.
 



 You will work both operationally and project-based, with the opportunity to gradually take on greater responsibility within forecasting, reporting, and the development of models and ways of working. The role suits someone who thrives in an entrepreneurial environment with a fast pace, where new questions and projects continuously arise, giving you the opportunity to grow alongside the business.
 



  





**Your responsibilities** 



* Support financial monitoring, analysis, and reporting across the Areim group
* Contribute to quarterly and annual closing processes, as well as internal reporting
* Develop, maintain, and enhance forecasting and financial models
* Prepare and review reporting materials for partners, the board, and external stakeholders
* Analyse profit and loss, balance sheet, cash flow, and liquidity
* Manage transactions, valuations, and liquidity flows related to holding companies and investments
* Support reporting and ongoing communication with external stakeholder
* Contribute to improving financial processes, reporting, and follow-up routines
* Support the development of data and reporting solutions, including BI tools and Excel models



  





**Qualifications and suitable background** 



* University degree in finance, economics, or equivalent
* 2-5 years of relevant work experience, as a financial analyst or/and within audit
* Experience from fund, financial, or advisory environments is a plus
* Very strong Excel skills
* Interest in data, analysis, automation, and BI tools
* Strong analytical ability and interest in understanding the business behind the numbers
* Fluent in Swedish and good command of English, both spoken and written, as communication is conducted in both languages.



  





**Personal qualities** 




 We are looking for someone who is analytical, curious, and engaged, with a strong desire to understand both detail and the bigger picture. You are quick to learn and thrive in an environment where you take initiative, ask questions, and drive work forward in collaboration with others. To succeed in the role, we believe you work in a structured and careful manner, while also having high integrity and a strong sense of responsibility. You are humble and comfortable working across both operational and strategic tasks, and you take initiative and independently drive matters forward. Furthermore, you are able to manage complex structures and multiple parallel tasks simultaneously. You appreciate an entrepreneurial environment with a fast pace and constant change, and you thrive in close collaboration with colleagues where trust, openness, and team spirit are at the center.
 



  





**Your application** 




 Do you recognize yourself in this description? Gazella is supporting Areim in this recruitment process. Please apply for the position by clicking the “Apply”-button below. You are also welcome to contact the recruiter responsible, Julia Zovko, at
 
 julia.zovko@gazella.se
 
 . Interviews are conducted on an ongoing basis so please submit your application as soon as possible. We look forward to hearing from you.
 



  





**Why Areim?** 




 At Areim, culture is something we practise, not just express. Our purpose, Make better happen, is our driving force, and our core values guide both what we do and how we do it. With curiosity, care, and unwavering commitment, we enable people, capital, assets, communities, and the future to thrive.
 



  





 Are you ready to Make better happen? Join us.

title: Product Manager – Collaboration Services
description:
**Securitas Group** 




 Securitas is a world-leading safety and security solutions partner that helps make your world a safer place. By leveraging technology in partnership with our clients, we offer a broad portfolio of value-enhancing services and solutions integrated across the security value chain – from on-site services to advanced monitoring, comprehensive risk prediction and advisory services.
 



 With around 322 000 employees in 44 markets, our innovative, holistic approach with local and global expertise makes us a trusted business partner to many of the world’s best-known companies. Benefitting from almost nine decades of deep experience and guided by our values of integrity, vigilance, and helpfulness, we create sustainable value by helping our clients optimize their operations and protect what matters most - their people and assets.
 



  





**Securitas IT** 




 Securitas IT creates capabilities and powerful technology needed to fulfil Securitas business strategy to be client centric, data driven, and people focused. We transform, build and protect our IT landscape in a cost-efficient a sufficient way.
 



  





**About the role** 




 Securitas is on a global journey to strengthen how our people collaborate, communicate, and work securely in a modern digital workplace.
 



  





 As
 **Product Manager – Collaboration Services** 
 , you will play a key role in shaping and leading our global collaboration portfolio. You will have
 **end** 
 ‑
 **to** 
 ‑
 **end ownership** 
 of Collaboration Services, including
 **Microsoft 365 delivery** 
 and
 **enterprise communication and mass** 
 ‑
 **mailing tools** 
 , used daily across the Securitas Group.
 



  





 This is a
 **senior leadership role** 
 combining product strategy, people management, financial responsibility, and vendor management in a complex, international environment.
 



  





**What you will do** 




 As Product Manager for Collaboration Services, you will:
 



**Own product strategy and roadmap** 



* Define and drive the long‑term
 **vision, strategy, and roadmap** 
 for Collaboration Services.
* Balance innovation, user experience, security, cost efficiency, and operational stability.
* Own the product backlog and prioritization across the full product lifecycle (design, build, run).



**Lead Microsoft 365 and collaboration delivery** 



* Oversee the global delivery and evolution of
 **Microsoft 365 collaboration services** 
 (e.g. Exchange Online, Teams, SharePoint, OneDrive).
* Own enterprise email, messaging, and
 **mass** 
 ‑
 **mailing/communication platforms** 
 .
* Drive adoption of new Microsoft capabilities while managing evergreen changes and dependencies.



**Manage and develop people** 



* Lead a
 **distributed, multicultural team** 
 of engineers and service managers across Europe.
* Set clear goals, priorities, and ways of working.
* Build a high‑performing, engaged, and accountable team culture.



**Own financial planning and reporting** 



* Be accountable for
 **budget planning, forecasting, cost control, and financial reporting** 
 for the Collaboration Services portfolio.
* Ensure transparent cost models, consumption tracking, and value realization.
* Present financial status, risks, and opportunities to senior stakeholders.



**Manage vendors and partners** 



* Own relationships with strategic vendors and service partners.
* Ensure commercial performance, cost efficiency, and delivery according to SLAs and OLAs.
* Drive continuous improvement and contract optimization.



**Act as a key stakeholder interface** 



* Collaborate closely with global and regional IT, security, architecture, and business stakeholders.
* Communicate clearly around roadmap, priorities, risks, and outcomes.
* Ensure alignment between Collaboration Services and the broader Digital Workplace strategy.



**Ensure governance, security, and compliance** 



* Ensure services meet security, privacy, and regulatory requirements.
* Own escalation handling, risk mitigation, and crisis management within the product area.
* Maintain alignment with enterprise architecture and security standards.



  





**What we are looking for** 




 We are looking for a
 **Senior Product Leader** 
 with strong Microsoft experience and the ability to lead both strategically and operationally.
 



**You bring** 



* **10+ years of experience within IT environment** 
 with
 **significant experience** 
**as a** 
**Product Manager/Product Owner** 
 in enterprise IT or Digital Workplace environments.
* Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Business, Engineer or other related field.
* Deep experience with the
 **Microsoft ecosystem** 
 , especially Microsoft 365 collaboration services.
* Proven experience in
 **people leadership** 
 , including managing senior specialists and distributed teams.
* Strong background in
 **financial planning, budgeting, and reporting** 
 .
* Experience managing
 **vendors and external partners** 
 .
* Ability to operate effectively in a
 **large, global, matrix** 
 ‑
 **structured organization** 
 .
* Fluency in
 **English.**



**You are** 



* A confident and mature leader with strong stakeholder management skills.
* Strategic, yet pragmatic and delivery‑focused.
* Financially literate and data‑driven.
* Comfortable making decisions in complex, high‑impact environments.
* A clear communicator who can engage both technical experts and senior leaders.



  





**Working conditions** 




 We are currently welcoming applications from candidates based in
 **Stockholm or Warsaw** 
 (with preference for candidates living in Stockholm).
 



  





 Regardless of the location, hybrid working model applies and you will be expected to work from our office at least
 **2 days per week** 
 .
 



  





 Additionally, the role involves occasional travel a few times per year (around 2-4 times).
 



  





**What we offer** 




 At Securitas we believe in doing the right thing and doing it well. For our customers and our employees. Our employees come from all walks of life and bring with them many talents and perspectives. We aim for diverse representation throughout the company, and we are committed to equal pay, safe working conditions, gender balance and an inclusive work environment with a wide range of skills and development opportunities.
 



  





**If this sounds like the right next step in your professional career, don't hesitate and apply!**

title: Media Solutions Engineer (Paid Search/SEM)
description:
At Precis, we are a group of friendly experts in media buying, analytics & marketing science, creative and technology with a joint mission to help great companies thrive in the digital landscape.
   

  

 Are you curious to learn more about us? Read more about us here. For insights into what life is like at Precis and a peek at the perks of working with us, visit this page. In our Handbook, you can get a neat summary of who we are, our culture and our offices. In this document, we have summarised what our recruitment process looks like. Take a look!
   

  

*We are committed to building an inclusive and fair workplace that attracts talent and advances its people, regardless of their background, experiences or perspectives. We believe that a diverse set of minds will continue to contribute to our innovative culture and our ability to reinvent ourselves constantly.*
**Role Overview**
 The Media Solutions Engineer (MSE) is a critical hybrid role that operates at the intersection of media strategy, business acumen, and technical development. This position is responsible for designing, building, and deploying custom, robust technical solutions that directly enhance client media performance and secure long-term agency partnership ("stickiness"). The MSE transforms complex media challenges into scalable, code-based automations and integrations.
   

  

**What You Will Be Doing 💼**
* Innovation-led Strategy: Lead the development of technical solutions that combine deep SEM expertise with programming to solve complex channel challenges and drive performance innovation.
* Advanced Tooling & Automation: Build and maintain high-impact tools that automate repetitive workflows and enable sophisticated search strategies at scale.
* Data Architecture & Integration: Architect and implement robust integrations between core platforms (Google Ads) and client data environments (BigQuery) to enable advanced functionality.
* Rapid Prototyping: Transition quickly from concept to code, developing working prototypes, such as a return-rate prediction model or inventory bidding strategies to address immediate client needs and test new SEM hypotheses.
* Technical Auditing & Performance: Conduct deep-dive audits of SEM account structures and data flows to identify technical inefficiencies, proposing scalable, code-based solutions that enhance delivery and account health.
* Strategic Workshops: Facilitate action-oriented consulting sessions with clients and internal teams to translate high-level search goals into tangible technical roadmaps and automated workflows.
* Technical Ownership & Reliability: Take full responsibility for the stability and performance of critical operational components, including feed management and custom scripts, ensuring they provide "technical insurance" for the client's media spend.
* Knowledge Scaling & Mentorship: Mentor specialists on technical SEM tactics and document best practices to build a repository of reusable solutions and foster a culture of continuous technical learning.


**What We Are Looking For ✔️**
* Experience: 4+ years of experience in the digital marketing ecosystem.
* SEM Technical Expertise: Comprehensive hands-on experience with SEM and feed management tools.
* Scripting & Automation: Ability to write and debug code (Python, JavaScript/Node.js, SQL) specifically for marketing automation and data manipulation.
* Platform Stack: Strong working knowledge of Google Ads Scripts, Google Cloud services (BigQuery, Cloud Run), and API integrations for marketing platforms.
* Analytical Mindset: Expertise in using GA4 and BigQuery to analyse large datasets and turn those insights into automated optimisation logic.
* Product Mindset: Ability to build solutions following internal standards, ensuring tools can be productized or integrated into internal platforms like Alvie.
* Strategic Communication: Excellent skills in presenting technical solutions to C-level stakeholders, turning complex data narratives into clear business initiatives.
* Self-Leadership: Highly self-driven, proactive, and able to manage complex technical projects independently.
* Emerging Tech Curiosity: A forward-leaning mindset regarding AI/Machine Learning applications within performance marketing.


**What We Offer 🌟**
*You can read our full offering in our* 
*handbook* 
*.*
* An intense learning environment where you will be able to grow and develop your skills and passions alongside some of the brightest minds in the industry.
* A culture that is built on collaboration, trust and innovation. We value having fun together - that’s why we also have regular afterworks, team events and a yearly festival.
* Transparent salary bands - check it out below on our site!
* 30 days paid leave, flexible public holidays, parental pay, a solid pension scheme and key insurance. Our different offices also offer different perks such as massage, health care contributions, free book orders, healthy snacks & great coffee, EAP programs and your choice of PC or Mac.
* A diverse environment with a high focus on inclusion and belonging. We speak 46 native languages around Precis, and our internal inclusion score is 4.5 out of 5.


 To make things as smooth as possible, please ensure your CV is in English.
   

  

**We update all candidates on their applications by email. If you don't receive an email from us, please check your spam folder.**

title: Manager, Pre-Sales and Solution Engineering - Nordics
description:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.
   

  

**The Team:**
 The Celonis
 **Value Engineering (VE)** 
 team is focused on managing the entire customer value lifecycle, from sales through value realization, renewal and expansion. As a
 **Value Engineer Manager** 
 , you will lead a dynamic team who come from a variety of backgrounds to identify, frame and deliver value to our customers.
   

  

**The Role:**
 As a
 **Pre-sales and Solution Engineering (Value Engineering) Manager** 
 , you will lead a team of Value Engineers and drive end-to-end customer value journeys, landing and expanding existing customers (ACV growth) and renewals (renewal rate). You will work closely with the Celonis GTM Leadership Teams and be responsible for the end-to-end value journey for all customers within your region/vertical, supporting customers to achieve their strategic goals and realize significant value using Celonis Process Intelligence Platform. You are a trusted advisor to the Celonis Executive Team, supporting their engagements with strategic customers and senior stakeholders.
   

  

 As a leader in one of the fastest-growing tech companies globally, you will bring together deep process and industry knowledge with a proven consulting skill set and strong analytical abilities. You will foster a positive and supportive work environment for your teams, promoting employee satisfaction and career growth.
   

  

**The work you’ll do:**
**Identify & Frame Value For Prospects/Customers**
* Own the end-to-end customer value journey, including responsibility for landing, expanding, adoption and renewing accounts
* Translate customers’ strategic objectives into high-impact Celonis use cases and evangelize Process Intelligence across prospects and customers
* Communicate the value opportunity and strategic roadmap to C-Level executives in partnership with sales leadership and Celonis executives
* Serve as an executive sponsor across the customer portfolio and solidify Celonis as a strategic business transformation platform for our customers


**Create & Scale Value Across The Portfolio**
* Lead customers through the Celonis Value Journey, support the deployment of new features, apps, and products across the customer base, and advance strategic growth programs in pursuit of value creation
* Ensure customer success and architect improvement measures and manage change within the customer base, while pioneering new methodologies within Celonis’ Value Engineering organisation
* Direct Celonis Value Engineering, Services and Ecosystem resources through the Celonis Customer Value Journey, creating durable value for our customers
* Take ownership and build strategic customer relationships as an executive sponsor
* Host QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews) and EBRs (Executive Business Reviews) to communicate results and realized value to senior management and C-level executives


**Grow & Lead Celonis Talent**
* Recruit the next-generation of Celonis Value Engineers, while setting a high-standard for candidate excellence
* Develop world-class talent through hands-on engagement, continuous feedback, and active mentorship
* Lead by example and consistently demonstrate the Celonis Leadership Principles (lead with purpose, facilitate greatness, embrace difference, and cultivate balance)
* Effectively allocate Celonis resources to deliver on company sales, renewals, value, and adoption targets
* Communicate success and risks proactively within the organization


**The qualifications you need:**
* Domain Expertise: Demonstrate your business domain expertise, such as in Finance or Supply Chain as well as experience in strategic transformation initiatives (e.g. Shared Services Transformation or System Transformation) and ability to build a compelling value roadmap for the customer
* Industry Expertise: Demonstrate a deep understanding of industry trends and strategic challenges of the C-Suite and build an integrated Celonis Process Intelligence roadmap
* Product Expertise: Experience with guiding customer’s IT teams throughout their Celonis Process Intelligence journey, demonstrating hands-on the power of Process Intelligence and proven ability to accelerate value realization by building dashboards, Apps, Action Flows
* Project Management: Plan and manage project scope, expectations and timelines. Leverage partners from the Celonis Ecosystem wherever possible.
* Executive Stakeholder Management: Experience in preparing and communicating results to stakeholders and management, both internally and externally.
* Value Selling Methodology: Demonstrate experience of following the Celonis value selling methodology alongside the customer journey: including discovery, qualification, value assessment and PoV’s, and demonstrate the full value potential of Celonis Process Intelligence
* Value Realization: Proven track record of identifying and prioritizing use cases, implementing improvement measures and becoming a change agent for the customer by establishing an operating model and training users for the customer to realize value and renew/expand their subscription with Celonis
* Sales & Commercial Acumen: Demonstrate ability to qualify all VE engagements, do discovery with customers directly, steer/allocate resources efficiently, and champion our value selling methodology with our customers.


***Leadership Skills:** 
 Proven ability to lead by example, including being on the front line with your teams, hands-on in all aspects and a role model for the organization.*
**What Celonis can offer you:**
* Pioneer Innovation: Work with the global leader in Process Mining and the Process Intelligence Graph to shape the future of AI-driven business operations.
* Ownership from Day 1: Every full-time Celonaut is an owner, receiving Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and merit-based refresh grants.
* Unrivaled Family Support: Benefit from our inclusive parental leave policy—24 weeks of fully paid leave for primary carers and 12 weeks for supporting carers, available from your first day of employment.
* Work-Life Integration: Enjoy Unlimited PTO (in applicable regions) and generous PTO globally, as well as a flexible hybrid work model that balances remote focus with vibrant office collaboration.
* Continuous Growth: Elevate your skills through our 70-20-10 learning framework, mentorship programs, and access to a dedicated learning platform.
* Holistic Well-being: Prioritize your health with subsidized Wellhub memberships, mental health counseling, and dedicated Wellness Weeks that prioritize work/life balance.
* Drive Sustainability: Participate in annual Impact Days, where you receive paid time off to volunteer for community and environmental causes with your local office, or virtually.
* Global Inclusion & Belonging: Find community through our Inclusion Think Tank and participate in our annual Inclusion Days, ensuring every voice is heard and valued.
* Value-Driven Impact: Join a mission-led organization where our core values—Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future—drive every decision.


**About Us:**
 Celonis makes processes work — for people, companies, and the planet. Powered by process mining and AI, the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform integrates process data and business context to create a living digital twin of business operations. We enable thousands of companies worldwide to understand how their business actually runs and, together with their partners, build intelligent solutions that transform and continuously improve the way they operate — unlocking billions in value. Celonis is headquartered in Munich, Germany, and New York City, USA, with more than 20 offices worldwide.
   

  

 Get familiar with the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform by watching this video.
   

  

**Celonis Inclusion Statement:**
 At Celonis, we believe our people make us who we are and that “The Best Team Wins”. We know that the best teams are made up of people who bring different perspectives to the table. And when everyone feels included, able to speak up and knows their voice is heard - that's when creativity and innovation happen.
   

  

**Your Privacy:**
 Any information you submit to Celonis as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Celonis’ Accessibility and Candidate Notices
   

  

 By submitting this application, you confirm that you agree to the storing and processing of your personal data by Celonis as described in our Privacy Notice for the Application and Hiring Process.
   

  

 Please be aware of common job offer scams, impersonators and frauds. Learn more here.

title: DevOps Engineer till utvecklingsteam
description:
Är du en skicklig DevOps Engineer som trivs i bryggpunkten mellan utveckling och drift? Hos oss får du en nyckelroll i att forma den tekniska basplattan för våra förmånståg. Vi söker dig som vill kombinera din tekniska expertis med ett starkt driv att stötta andra, där du genom automation och stabila CI/CD-flöden möjliggör smidig mjukvaruleverans i en verksamhet som gör skillnad på riktigt.
   

  

**Om jobbet**
 Som DevOps Engineer hos oss spelar du en avgörande roll i att forma och förvalta den tekniska basplattan för ditt team och tåg. Du tillhör ett tvärfunktionellt team men verkar också i en tät DevOps-kontext tillsammans med kollegor som delar din expertis. Här blir du länken som får utveckling och drift att smälta samman till en effektiv helhet.
   

  

 Dina ansvarsområden:
   

  

 Ditt fokus ligger på att bygga, underhålla och ständigt förbättra den infrastruktur och de arbetsflöden som krävs för en modern mjukvaruleverans. Du förväntas:
   

  

 Driva automation: I samarbete med plattformsteamen tar du fram innovativa lösningar för automation och releaseprocesser.
   

  

 Säkerställa kvalitet: Du ansvarar för systemuppgraderingar och ser till att våra riktlinjer för CI/CD inte bara efterlevs, utan också hålls uppdaterade mot branschstandard.
   

  

 Stötta utvecklingsteamet: En stor del av din vardag innebär att agera teknisk möjliggörare och hjälpa utvecklare att röja hinder i deras processer.
   

  

 Helhetsansvar: Vi arbetar efter principen "You build it, you run it". Det innebär att du är med hela vägen – från att bygga lösningen till att ansvara för dess drift och stabilitet. Beredskap ingår under ett antal kvällar och helger per år.
   

  

 Vem är du i teamet?
   

  

 Du trivs med att arbeta både självständigt och i nära samverkan med andra. Eftersom du besitter expertkunskaper som ofta saknas i andra delar av organisationen, blir din pedagogiska ådra och din vilja att dela med dig av din kompetens avgörande. Du är en nyckelperson för att vi ska kunna leverera smidigt, säkert och effektivt.
   

  

**Din profil**
 För att lyckas i rollen som DevOps Engineer ser vi att du är en självgående person som tar stort ägarskap för dina ansvarsområden. Du har förmågan att själv strukturera ditt arbete och driva komplexa processer framåt, även när förutsättningarna förändras. Du är proaktiv och nyfiken, med ett starkt fokus på att driva innovation. Genom att utforska nya tekniska lösningar och omsätta kreativa idéer i praktiken bidrar du aktivt till att höja vår effektivitet och utveckla våra arbetssätt.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har en god analytisk och problemlösande förmåga. Du trivs i en föränderlig teknisk miljö och har en naturlig fallenhet för att analysera problem och föreslå stabila, skalbara lösningar.
   

  

 Du är lyhörd, har god samarbets- och kommunikationsförmåga. Du drivs av en vilja att hjälpa andra och anstränger dig för att leverera lösningar som underlättar för dina kollegor och teamet.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som har:
   

  

* Slutförd yh-utbildning inom DevOps eller likvärdigt.
* Flytande i svenska och i tal och skrift och goda kunskaper i engelska.
* Flerårig erfarenhet av att arbeta med Linux-baserade plattformar och infrastrukturmiljöer, gärna i DevOps- eller DevEx-team.
* Flerårig erfarenhet av teknisk felsökning och incidenthantering i komplexa, heterogena IT-miljöer.
* God teknisk förståelse och erfarenhet av att samarbeta med tekniskt inriktade team, arkitekter och andra intressenter.


 Det är meriterande om du har:
   

  

* Arbetserfarenhet av systemadministration i en Linuxbaserad produktionsmiljö.
* Erfarenhet av att konfigurera/utveckla pipelines för CI/CD.
* Erfarenhet av Jenkins.
* Arbetserfarenhet av containerteknik med Docker och Kubernetes i produktionsmiljö.


**Vi erbjuder**
 Ett myndighetsuppdrag som är viktigt på riktigt! En arbetsplats där vi värnar om att våra medarbetare ska ha en god balans mellan arbete och fritid. Vi erbjuder flexibel arbetstid och möjlighet att jobba delvis hemifrån. Som statligt anställd har du även goda anställningsvillkor och ett förmånspaket med friskvård, lediga klämdagar samt goda semester- och pensionsvillkor.
   

  

 Du kan läsa mer om våra förmåner och hur det är att jobba hos oss här.
   

  

**Bra att veta**
 Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning på heltid och vi tillämpar 6 månaders provanställning.
   

  

 Placeringsort är Stockholm eller Luleå.
   

  

 I tjänsten ingår viss beredskap, kvälls- och helgarbete. Några tillfällen per år som delas mellan kollegor med samma roll. Vid placering i Luleå förekommer vissa resor till Stockholm, 4-6 tillfällen per år.
   

  

 Anställningen kan komma att placeras i säkerhetsklass.
   

  

**Din ansökan**
 Vi jobbar kompetensbaserat i hela processen och är måna om en inkluderande, rättvis, objektiv och kandidatvänlig process. Därför ber vi dig att besvara ett antal urvalsfrågor istället för att be om personligt brev.
   

  

 Vi kommer att använda oss av arbetspsykologiska tester.
   

  

 Har du skyddade personuppgifter kontakta rekryterande chef som hjälper dig med din ansökan.
   

  

 Vi strävar efter en jämn könsfördelning och ser mångfald som en styrka och välkomnar därför sökande med olika bakgrund.
   

  

 Inför rekryteringsarbetet har vi tagit ställning till annonskanaler och marknadsföring. Vi undanber oss därför ytterligare kontakt med media-/annonsförsäljare, rekryteringssajter och liknande.
   

  

*Pensionsmyndigheten ansvarar för den allmänna pensionen och informerar om hela pensionen. Varje år betalar vi ut cirka 415 miljarder kronor till 2,3 miljoner pensionärer, genomför cirka 911 000 fondbyten och skickar ut 8 miljoner orange kuvert. Vi har ca 25 miljoner digitala kundkontakter per år. Vi är cirka 1700 medarbetare på åtta orter, från Luleå i norr till Halmstad i söder.*
*Vi gör pensionen enklare.*
 Anställningsform: tillsvidare med provanställning | Anställningens omfattning: heltid | Antal lediga befattningar: 2 | Sysselsättningsgrad: 100% | Ort: Stockholm | Län: Stockholms län | Land: Sweden | Referensnummer: PSL 2026-75 | Kontakt: Alexandra Bjelkholm 010-4542520, | Facklig företrädare: Michael Karlsson, ST 010-4543427, Anna-Karin Åhrman Cortelius, Saco-s 010-4543914, | Publicerat: 2026-04-23 | Sista ansökningsdag: 2026-05-14

title: Information & Workflow Specialist
description:
****What we do at Blykalla****
*Blykalla is a Swedish deep-tech company at the forefront of the nuclear industry, pioneering the development of small modular reactors that provide clean, safe, and reliable energy. Thanks to lead-cooling, we achieve high safety in a very compact format, allowing for a simple and cost-effective design that can be mass-produced. Our unique reactor design will provide safe and green baseload energy that perfectly complements solar and wind powe* 
 r.
   

  

****What you’ll get to do****
 You support in defining and continuously improving the standards, tools, and governance framework for engineering information management across Engineering. As the framework matures, you take ownership of its operational execution ensuring that documents, tags, equipment data, review cycles, and work packages are structured, traceable, and connected to the way the plant is designed, built, and commissioned. You implement and maintain standards for tools, metadata, templates, and workflows and make sure the organization works through system-driven and automated processes rather than manual movement of files between folders. You turn information and document management into an enabling delivery function by automating RFI, design review, supplier review, and issue management workflows, and by integrating documents and work processes into BIM and the three-dimensional model. You help build a digital thread that supports design, procurement, construction, commissioning, and long-term operations.
   

  

****Who’ll you’ll get to work with****
*The Information & Workflow Specialist sits in the Plant EngineeringTeam within Blykalla, and reports to the Manager of Plant Engineering. We are a diverse team that is passionate about our work and Blykalla.*
****Who you are****
* 10+ years of experience in document controls or engineering information management, ideally from heavy industry, oil and gas, manufacturing, or power generation.
* Strong command of document structures, metadata, review workflows, work packaging, model integration, and automation — and ability to drive consistent adoption across teams.
* Able to anticipate issues impacting schedule, data quality, handover readiness, or cost — and act before they become critical.
* Balances governance, automation, and practical delivery.
* Acts as a role model and builds trust across functions by being transparent, dependable, and solution-oriented.


****Meritorious skills****
* Experience coaching users and raising document controls capability across an engineering organisation.
* Background in contributing to tool strategy, resource planning, and ways of working across engineering.
* Nuclear or other highly regulated industry experience.


**Your role covers the following areas**
 You are responsible for contributing to Blykalla’s engineering capability by incorporating the areas below into your daily work:
   

  

**Information standards and work packaging**
* Execute and coordinate the document controls scope for a plant area, project, or cross-functional work package.
* Contribute to defining and then implement document, equipment, tag, naming, revision, and metadata standards and make sure templates support consistent use.
* Apply and maintain holistic work package structures that link documents, model views, tags, equipment data, review states, and downstream handover needs.


**Workflow design and automation**
* Implement and maintain workflows for RFIs, design reviews, supplier reviews, comment resolution, approvals, issue handling, and handover.
* Configure and operate platforms such as Aras, so routing, permissions, status handling, notifications, and reporting happen through the system rather than shared folders.
* Deploy and maintain templates, forms, dashboards, and automation rules that reduce manual touchpoints and improve traceability.
* Review workflow performance, identify bottlenecks or data quality issues early, and make sure information is fit for use and fit for issue.


**Systems and model integration**
* Support integration implementation of documents, work processes, equipment data, and tag data with BIM and the three-dimensional model so engineering information can be found in context.
* Support model-based reviews and make sure governed documents, tags, and equipment references are linked to the right model objects and plant areas.
* Coordinate with discipline leads, BIM, and configuration stakeholders to maintain and strengthen the digital thread across plant and product disciplines.


**Suppliers and external partners**
* Prepare requirements for suppliers and external partners covering document standards, metadata, review cycles, handover rules, and system usage.
* Review supplier and contractor information flows and make sure vendor documents, comments, and responses follow the required workflows and standards.
* Represent document controls in meetings with partners, software vendors, contractors, and internal stakeholders when document control process are needed.


**Project delivery and operational support**
* Escalate issues with broken workflows, missing metadata, inconsistent tags or equipment references, and incomplete work packages early and propose practical mitigation actions.
* Provide timely support to design reviews, comment resolution, RFI workflows, issue management, release for issue, and turnover readiness for construction and commissioning.


**Team development and ways of working**
* Mentor users, train teams, and improve methods, templates, checklists, standard reports, and adoption of good document controls practice.
* Help build a document controls function that can scale with Blykalla’s project needs through automation, standardization, BIM integration, and repeatable digital work packages.


**Typical deliverables**
 Document control plans, tool configurations, metadata and numbering standards, template libraries, workflow maps, design review and RFI workflows, dashboards, supplier onboarding guidance, work package definitions, BIM and model linking rules, information handover requirements, and improvement roadmaps.
   

  

****Main metrics for this role may include:****
* Workflow cycle time for design reviews, RFIs, comment closure, and issue resolution.
* Quality of metadata, templates, work package setup, and first-pass review readiness.
* Share of documents, tags, equipment records, and work packages with complete, correct, and traceable metadata and model links.
* Reduction in manual touchpoints and turnaround time through automation and system-driven workflows.
* Predictability of information release, supplier review closure, and handover readiness for construction and commissioning.
* Contributions to team capability through training, coaching, and adoption of document controls standards, automation, and tool improvement.


 If you are not sure that you are 100% qualified but are up to the challenge, we encourage you to apply.
   

  

****Location****
 This position is for our office in Stockholm.
   

  

****So, what do you think?****
 Don't miss the chance to be at the forefront of revolutionizing nuclear power. Join our team as Information & Workflow Specialist and help shape the energy landscape of tomorrow. Apply today with your resume and a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and achievements.
   

  

 We are proud to foster an inclusive workplace free from discrimination. We strongly believe that diversity of experience, perspectives, and background will lead to a better environment for our employees and a better product. This is something we value deeply and we encourage everyone to be a part of changing the way the world thinks about power supply!
   

  

**We do not accept applications via email.**
 We are not open to external help from recruitment agencies.

title: Research Assistant in Zoology
description:
**The Department of Zoology** 
 of Stockholm University includes ca. 80 employees, including researchers, PhD students, and administrative staff. Research and education at the department occurs in an international environment. Outstanding and high impact research is conducted in a variety of fields, including evolutionary biology, molecular and population genetics, genomics, conservation, and behaviour.
   

  

**SciLifeLab** 
 is a premier Swedish national research infrastructure dedicated to advanced molecular biosciences. In Stockholm, SciLifeLab is located on Campus Solna, where research groups from Stockholm University, the Royal Institute of Technology, and the Karolinska Institute conduct internationally outstanding research in the life sciences.
   

  

 More information about us, please visit: the Department of Zoology.
   

  

 Main responsibilities
   

  

 The position is associated with the research group of Mafalda Sousa Ferreira at the Department of Zoology. The main focus of this research position is to produce and analyze UMI-4C data from fibroblast cell lines for the functional validation of pigmentation genes in non-model species, specifically hares. The group also conducts research into the genetic basis of adaptation in other systems, including ptarmigan, and ongoing research is producing whole-genome data for these animals at large scale.
   

  

 We Are Looking For a Candidate Holding a Master’s Degree In Molecular Biology, Biotechnology Or a Related Field. The Successful Candidate Will
   

  

* Conduct laboratory work including mammalian cell culture, UMI-4C experiments, and whole-genome library preparation, as well as all other steps associated with these protocols.
* Contribute to the analysis of other genomic datasets, potentially including long-read (nanopore) sequencing or transcriptomics data.
* Work independently and collaboratively within an international research environment.


 Read more
   

  

 Qualification requirements
   

  

**Required Qualifications**
* A Master’s degree in molecular biology, biotechnology or a related field, completed by or near the time of employment.
* Previous experience with cell culture of mammalian cell lines.
* Previous experience with the UMI-4C protocol.
* General experience with all steps of molecular work related to genomic library preparations.
* Experience with genomic data analysis and working in a high-performance computing (HPC) environment, including tools for sequence alignment, genome assembly or population genetics.


**Meriting Qualifications**
* Experience in research on mammalian pigmentation, including seasonal color change.
* Familiarity with nanopore sequencing or transcriptomics data analysis.
* Familiarity with transposable element biology or repeat annotation.


 In the employment process, special emphasis will be placed on documented research expertise in the relevant areas described above.
   

  

**About The Employment**
 The employment is a fixed-term appointment of 4 months, with the possibility of extension, at 100% employment. The start date is at the end of May/beginning of June 2026, by agreement. The place of employment is SciLifeLab, Solna. Stockholm University applies individual salary setting.
   

  

 We offer
   

  

 With us, you will experience the dynamic interaction between higher education and research that makes Stockholm University an exciting and creative environment. You will work in an international environment and get favourable conditions. The university is located in the National City Park with good transport links to the city.
   

  

 Stockholm University strives to be a workplace free from discrimination and with equal opportunities for all.
   

  

 Contact
   

  

 Further information about the position can be obtained from Mafalda Sousa Ferreira, mafalda.ferreira@zoologi.su.se.
   

  

 Application
   

  

 Apply for the position at Stockholm University’s recruitment system. It is the responsibility of the applicant to ensure that the application is complete and that it is submitted before the deadline.
   

  

 The instructions for applicants are available at: How to apply for a position.
   

  

 Stockholm University contributes to the development of sustainable democratic society through knowledge, enlightenment and the pursuit of truth.
   

  

 Type of employment Temporary position Contract type Full time Salary Individual salary setting Number of positions 1 Full-time equivalent 100 % City Stockholm County Stockholms län Country Sweden Reference number SU FV-1650-26 Union representative ST/OFR ST/OFR, 08162000, st@st.su.seSaco-S Saco-S, 08162000, saco@saco.su.seSeko Seko, 0770457900, sekodirekt@seko.se Published 12.May.2026 Last application date 26.May.2026

title: Risk Data Modeller to Finance, Risk, and Compliance | SEB, Stockholm
description:
SEB is more than just a bank with an IT function. We are a progressive tech company powered by innovation and fueled by collaboration. From COBOL to AI and everything in between, we offer you an impressive tech stack and several technology paths. Together we're innovating for a sustainable society and building the tech arena of the future, a welcoming space for everyone.
   

  

 Finance, Risk, and Compliance are undergoing a major transformation—driven by business and regulatory changes as well as significant shifts in architectural design. We are therefore looking for a Risk Data Modeller with experience in data modelling to develop an integrated data architecture that meets regulatory requirements and business needs. You will be a vital part of our community driving the Finance, Risk, and Compliance agenda in the bank. Are you ready to take on a new challenge? If so, this opportunity is for you!
   

  

**About the role** 
**:**
 As a Risk Data Modeller in our team, you will take responsibility for the data architecture within the risk area. Your work will involve a high degree of complexity, both from a business and data perspective as well as from a people and process perspective. You’ll work closely with business stakeholders and architects to:
   

  

* Capture and interpret business and regulatory requirements driven by our major initiatives.
* Develop an integrated data architecture for Risk that supports all risk types, ensuring data is consistent, integrated, traceable, and fit for regulatory and business use, while connecting to the domain‑wide data architecture.
* Develop business information and logical data models that align with business needs and integrate with business and application architectures, supporting metadata capture, consistency, lineage, and traceability across the data landscape.
* Support the definition of business terms, and ensure they are correctly represented, consistently used, and traceable across models, data flows, and solutions.
* Ensure compliance with BCBS 239, Basel/CRR, IFRS, and other applicable regulatory requirements, and alignment with the domain architecture.
* Collaborate with stakeholders from the risk organisation, data management professionals, and architects across Finance, Risk, and Compliance.


**To thrive in this role, we believe you have:**
* Strong professional background in risk management within a banking or financial services (e.g., credit, market, counterparty, or liquidity risk).
* Competence in developing ontologies, business information models, and logical data models (including normalised and dimensional models), as well as data definitions, data mapping specifications, and data flows.
* Good understanding of data warehouse architectures and methodologies, and familiarity with the Data Mesh framework and data products.
* Knowledge of regulatory and data management requirements in Finance and Risk areas, and how data architecture enables compliance and governance.
* Experience with BCBS 239 (Risk Data Aggregation and Risk Reporting, RDARR) would be an advantage.
* Strong problem-solving and critical thinking skills.
* Ability to collaborate effectively with a positive, solution‑oriented mindset.
* Excellent communication and documentation skills to convey complex ideas effectively to both business and IT professionals.
* Fluency in English.


**What we offer:**
* A key role in one of SEB’s most challenging areas
* Innovative working environment with highly motivated colleagues
* Agile and modern ways of working
* Hybrid work model, meaning remote work is possible up to 2 days/week
* Attractive compensation and access to SEB staff banking with exclusive benefits


**Do you want to be part of SEB?**
 It is our fundamental belief that inclusion and diversity is crucial to our future success. We strive to have an inclusive, value-driven culture where employees feel valued, respected, and involved irrespective of who they are, what they believe or where they come from.
   

  

 Since we select candidates continuously, feel free to send in your application today via the link in this ad, but no later than May 30th.
   

  

 Please be aware that our final candidates undergo background checks, a process that includes for example identity control, verification of qualifications, credit checks, company engagements and history of crime. In some cases, we also apply random drug checks. During employment, employees within SEB can also expect to undergo recurring background checks.
   

  

 SEB Sweden has a redeployment responsibility, why this position might be covered by internal redeployment
   

  

**Learn more about working at SEB** 
 on our Career website or through our Career podcasts.
   

  

 https://sebgroup.com/career
   

  

 https://bit.ly/SEBcareer
   

  

**Experience keywords:**
 Risk, Quant, Data Architecture, Data Modelling, Conceptual Modelling, Business Information Modelling, Logical Modelling, Data Warehousing, Data Mesh, Data Product, Ontology, Taxonomy, Agile, SAFe

title: Manager, Value Engineering - Nordics
description:
Celonis is the global leader in Process Intelligence and the pioneer of Process Mining technology. As one of the world’s fastest-growing enterprise SaaS companies, we are changemakers pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. We invest heavily in advanced AI capabilities—specifically our Process Intelligence Graph—to turn data insights into immediate business action. We believe there is a massive opportunity to unlock global productivity and sustainability by placing intelligence at the core of every business process. Join our mission to make processes work for people, companies, and the planet.
   

  

**The Team:**
 The Celonis
 **Value Engineering (VE)** 
 team is focused on managing the entire customer value lifecycle, from sales through value realization, renewal and expansion. As a
 **Value Engineer Manager** 
 , you will lead a dynamic team who come from a variety of backgrounds to identify, frame and deliver value to our customers.
   

  

**The Role:**
 As a
 **Pre-sales and Solution Engineering (Value Engineering) Manager** 
 , you will lead a team of Value Engineers and drive end-to-end customer value journeys, landing and expanding existing customers (ACV growth) and renewals (renewal rate). You will work closely with the Celonis GTM Leadership Teams and be responsible for the end-to-end value journey for all customers within your region/vertical, supporting customers to achieve their strategic goals and realize significant value using Celonis Process Intelligence Platform. You are a trusted advisor to the Celonis Executive Team, supporting their engagements with strategic customers and senior stakeholders.
   

  

 As a leader in one of the fastest-growing tech companies globally, you will bring together deep process and industry knowledge with a proven consulting skill set and strong analytical abilities. You will foster a positive and supportive work environment for your teams, promoting employee satisfaction and career growth.
   

  

**The work you’ll do:**
**Identify & Frame Value For Prospects/Customers**
* Own the end-to-end customer value journey, including responsibility for landing, expanding, adoption and renewing accounts
* Translate customers’ strategic objectives into high-impact Celonis use cases and evangelize Process Intelligence across prospects and customers
* Communicate the value opportunity and strategic roadmap to C-Level executives in partnership with sales leadership and Celonis executives
* Serve as an executive sponsor across the customer portfolio and solidify Celonis as a strategic business transformation platform for our customers


**Create & Scale Value Across The Portfolio**
* Lead customers through the Celonis Value Journey, support the deployment of new features, apps, and products across the customer base, and advance strategic growth programs in pursuit of value creation
* Ensure customer success and architect improvement measures and manage change within the customer base, while pioneering new methodologies within Celonis’ Value Engineering organisation
* Direct Celonis Value Engineering, Services and Ecosystem resources through the Celonis Customer Value Journey, creating durable value for our customers
* Take ownership and build strategic customer relationships as an executive sponsor
* Host QBRs (Quarterly Business Reviews) and EBRs (Executive Business Reviews) to communicate results and realized value to senior management and C-level executives


**Grow & Lead Celonis Talent**
* Recruit the next-generation of Celonis Value Engineers, while setting a high-standard for candidate excellence
* Develop world-class talent through hands-on engagement, continuous feedback, and active mentorship
* Lead by example and consistently demonstrate the Celonis Leadership Principles (lead with purpose, facilitate greatness, embrace difference, and cultivate balance)
* Effectively allocate Celonis resources to deliver on company sales, renewals, value, and adoption targets
* Communicate success and risks proactively within the organization


**The qualifications you need:**
* Domain Expertise: Demonstrate your business domain expertise, such as in Finance or Supply Chain as well as experience in strategic transformation initiatives (e.g. Shared Services Transformation or System Transformation) and ability to build a compelling value roadmap for the customer
* Industry Expertise: Demonstrate a deep understanding of industry trends and strategic challenges of the C-Suite and build an integrated Celonis Process Intelligence roadmap
* Product Expertise: Experience with guiding customer’s IT teams throughout their Celonis Process Intelligence journey, demonstrating hands-on the power of Process Intelligence and proven ability to accelerate value realization by building dashboards, Apps, Action Flows
* Project Management: Plan and manage project scope, expectations and timelines. Leverage partners from the Celonis Ecosystem wherever possible.
* Executive Stakeholder Management: Experience in preparing and communicating results to stakeholders and management, both internally and externally.
* Value Selling Methodology: Demonstrate experience of following the Celonis value selling methodology alongside the customer journey: including discovery, qualification, value assessment and PoV’s, and demonstrate the full value potential of Celonis Process Intelligence
* Value Realization: Proven track record of identifying and prioritizing use cases, implementing improvement measures and becoming a change agent for the customer by establishing an operating model and training users for the customer to realize value and renew/expand their subscription with Celonis
* Sales & Commercial Acumen: Demonstrate ability to qualify all VE engagements, do discovery with customers directly, steer/allocate resources efficiently, and champion our value selling methodology with our customers.


***Leadership Skills:** 
 Proven ability to lead by example, including being on the front line with your teams, hands-on in all aspects and a role model for the organization.*
**What Celonis can offer you:**
* Pioneer Innovation: Work with the global leader in Process Mining and the Process Intelligence Graph to shape the future of AI-driven business operations.
* Ownership from Day 1: Every full-time Celonaut is an owner, receiving Restricted Stock Units (RSUs) and merit-based refresh grants.
* Unrivaled Family Support: Benefit from our inclusive parental leave policy—24 weeks of fully paid leave for primary carers and 12 weeks for supporting carers, available from your first day of employment.
* Work-Life Integration: Enjoy Unlimited PTO (in applicable regions) and generous PTO globally, as well as a flexible hybrid work model that balances remote focus with vibrant office collaboration.
* Continuous Growth: Elevate your skills through our 70-20-10 learning framework, mentorship programs, and access to a dedicated learning platform.
* Holistic Well-being: Prioritize your health with subsidized Wellhub memberships, mental health counseling, and dedicated Wellness Weeks that prioritize work/life balance.
* Drive Sustainability: Participate in annual Impact Days, where you receive paid time off to volunteer for community and environmental causes with your local office, or virtually.
* Global Inclusion & Belonging: Find community through our Inclusion Think Tank and participate in our annual Inclusion Days, ensuring every voice is heard and valued.
* Value-Driven Impact: Join a mission-led organization where our core values—Live for Customer Value, The Best Team Wins, We Own It, and Earth Is Our Future—drive every decision.


**About Us:**
 Celonis makes processes work — for people, companies, and the planet. Powered by process mining and AI, the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform integrates process data and business context to create a living digital twin of business operations. We enable thousands of companies worldwide to understand how their business actually runs and, together with their partners, build intelligent solutions that transform and continuously improve the way they operate — unlocking billions in value. Celonis is headquartered in Munich, Germany, and New York City, USA, with more than 20 offices worldwide.
   

  

 Get familiar with the Celonis Process Intelligence Platform by watching this video.
   

  

**Celonis Inclusion Statement:**
 At Celonis, we believe our people make us who we are and that “The Best Team Wins”. We know that the best teams are made up of people who bring different perspectives to the table. And when everyone feels included, able to speak up and knows their voice is heard - that's when creativity and innovation happen.
   

  

**Your Privacy:**
 Any information you submit to Celonis as part of your application will be processed in accordance with Celonis’ Accessibility and Candidate Notices
   

  

 By submitting this application, you confirm that you agree to the storing and processing of your personal data by Celonis as described in our Privacy Notice for the Application and Hiring Process.
   

  

 Please be aware of common job offer scams, impersonators and frauds. Learn more here.

title: Product Manager - Streaming
description:
At Viaplay Group, we entertain millions of people every day through our streaming services, radio networks, and TV channels. We believe in the power of content not just as a way of telling stories and touching lives, but also expanding worlds.
   

  

 We’re looking for the best people to join us on our journey. Right now, we’re searching for a
 **Product Manager - Streaming** 
 in our
 **Stockholm** 
 office – are you ready to hit play on an exciting career change?
   

  

**The Role**
 We are now looking for a Product Manager to join our Streaming team - a cross-functional team of around eight engineers and specialists whose mission is to deliver the best possible stream at any given time.
   

  

 The team owns the backend that makes video streaming work at scale - content packaging, CDN delivery, play-services, and the VOD pipeline. Live streaming is a core part of the remit, from sports to news, and the team works closely with the Technical Operations Center to keep the experience smooth when it matters most.
   

  

 You will work closely with the development team and collaborate with other product managers, designers, data analysts and operations. Alongside this, you'll have strong support from engineering leadership, user researchers and product analysts to ensure we build the right things, measure real impact, and continuously improve the product for our viewers.
   

  

**What You’ll Do**
* Own and prioritise your team's roadmap based on customer value and business impact
* Lead the full product lifecycle from discovery and ideation to launch, validation and continuous improvement
* Initiate and drive your own product initiatives, as well as lead projects defined together with stakeholders
* Use qualitative insights, quantitative data and experimentation to guide decisions and validate outcomes
* Collaborate closely with your development team and align product, data, tech and business stakeholders around clear priorities and shared goals
* Champion live streaming quality - partnering with engineering and the Technical Operations Center to deliver low-latency, resilient experiences for sports broadcasts and live events
* Define and track streaming Quality of Experience metrics - startup errors, buffer ratio, join time - and use them to guide priorities


**What We’re Looking For**
 We understand you may feel confident ticking certain boxes more than others and that’s why we always keep an open mind in our recruitment process. But, in order to thrive in this role, we do believe you’ll have at least some experience in the following:
   

  

* 5+ years of experience as a Product Manager, Product Owner or Project Manager in digital products
* Strong experience in modern agile product discovery and impact-driven delivery
* Comfortable working with technical teams and curious about the streaming domain - you don't need to write code, but you understand (or want to) how things like CDNs, video packaging and streaming protocols work
* Data-informed decision-making - comfortable defining metrics, reading dashboards, and using data to challenge assumptions and guide priorities
* A confident communicator able to align diverse stakeholders and navigate complex organisations
* Structured, outcome-driven and motivated by measurable impact
* Comfortable working in fast-moving environments where priorities shift and direction evolves


**It’s a bonus if you have:** 
  

  

* Knowledge of video streaming protocols - HLS, DASH, CMAF, or low-latency variants (LL-HLS, LL-DASH)
* Familiarity with DRM systems - Widevine, FairPlay, PlayReady - and how content protection fits into the streaming stack
* Experience from subscription-based or consumer streaming services


 What we offer you
   

  

* We’ve got you covered! 30 days of paid vacation every year, an attractive pension and insurance scheme, and generous parental leave pay lift.
* Your wellbeing matters. We provide you with various wellbeing initiatives including wellness allowance.
* Flexibility that works. We follow a hybrid model with at least three days a week in the office, giving you the best mix of collaboration and focus time.
* A safe space to grow and up-skill. Our learning culture puts you in the driver’s seat of your own development.
