* Engagement with senior decision makers up to the C-level to articulate business goals and benefits.
* Proven efficiency working across multiple business units to achieve results.
* Track record of external communications e.g., conferences, meet-ups, online and social-media.


 F5 is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes diversity in the workplace.
   

  

 The Job Description is intended to be a general representation of the responsibilities and requirements of the job. However, the description may not be all-inclusive, and responsibilities and requirements are subject to change.
   

  

**Please note that F5 only contacts candidates through F5 email address (ending with @f5.com) or auto email notification from Workday (ending with f5.com or** 
**@myworkday.com)** 
**.**
**Equal Employment Opportunity**
 It is the policy of F5 to provide equal employment opportunities to all employees and employment applicants without regard to unlawful considerations of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, sensory, physical, or mental disability, marital status, veteran or military status, genetic information, or any other classification protected by applicable local, state, or federal laws. This policy applies to all aspects of employment, including, but not limited to, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, discipline, and termination. F5 offers a variety of reasonable accommodations for candidates. Requesting an accommodation is completely voluntary. F5 will assess the need for accommodations in the application process separately from those that may be needed to perform the job. Request by contacting accommodations@f5.com.

title: Principal Mechanical Engineer
description:
Working at CorPower Ocean you will be part of a world class team, determined to make big impact. We are on a mission to Power the Planet with clean electricity from ocean waves. We offer autonomy and flexibility, combined with personal responsibility. CorPower Ocean is a global leader in wave energy, with a technology that has shown game-changing performance. Your work will contribute to the global energy transition by unlocking one of the largest untapped sources of clean energy on earth. Since the start in 2012 CorPower has raised more than 115mEUR to bring our technology to market, supporting a growing commercial project pipeline.
 



  





**The Role** 




  





 As a Principal Mechanical Engineer at Corpower Ocean, you will provide technical leadership across the design, analysis, manufacturing, testing and certification of all mechanical systems and subsystems of the wave energy converter (WEC) and CorPack technologies. Based at our HQ in Stockholm, you will work closely with the Chief Engineer and CTO, using your strong grasp of fundamentals and industrial mechanical engineering experience to support and guide the delivery of the critical systems.
 



  





**Responsibilities** 




  




* Design of mechanical structures and mechanisms (from concept to release-for-production)
* Present to cross-disciplinary stakeholders during design reviews
* Perform structural analysis (hand calculation and FEA)
* Produce technical documentation for internal use & 3rd party verification and certification
* Perform failure mode, effects and criticality analysis for modules and system
* Use your experience to design systems and components to meet performance, schedule and budget targets
* Investigate new solutions to improve performance, reliability or manufacturability
* Interface with requirement, supply chain, manufacturing and field-testing teams
* Support and coach more junior engineers
* Support assembly, testing, and pilot installations in the field



  





**Skills** 




  




* You have a minimum of 10-12 years of relevant experience from designing mechanical systems, highly loaded structures and/or industrial equipment.
* You have a work history where you have successfully led multiple full product development cycles from design, build, test, deploy/release/produce for different projects.
* You have a broad technical knowledge and genuine interest in the different aspects of machinery and mechanical design. Knowledge of mooring, gear box technology, pneumatics, hydraulics, structural optimization, pressure vessel, fabrication, sensors, routings and/or electricity generation equipment is desirable.
* You have experience working with CAD e.g. Creo. Experience working within PLM tools and structural finite element analysis an advantage.
* Experience with offshore, marine, mining, or any other sectors with similar harsh and/or industrial environment requirements is preferred.
* You may have a BSc or a MSc. degree in mechanical engineering or related disciplines, but your personality, experience and interest in the field is the most important. You are proficient in English, both spoken and written. Your personality, experience and interest in the field are the most important and what is most highly valued at CorPower.



  





**As a person, you:** 




  




* are striving for excellence, put quality into everything you do
* are self-motivated and constantly seek the most efficient ways to work
* search for simple and sustainable solutions to difficult problems
* love to learn new things, and take pride in constantly developing your skills
* are persistent, knowing it takes stamina to reach pioneering goals
* see your tasks through to completion
* are comfortable in presenting your solutions internally and externally
* are data driven and strive to always back your points with solid analysis



  





 CorPower Ocean has a diverse team with many nationalities and backgrounds, all working towards the same goal: To Power the Planet with clean energy from ocean waves.

title: Regional Sales Manager, Sweden & Norway | Trauma Product Portfolio
description:
**The Job’s Mission:**
 At Stryker, we are looking for a talented, highly motivated sales leader who will be responsible for driving our
 **Trauma** 
 business in both
 **Sweden & Norway** 
 .
   

  

 Our innovative Trauma division offers market-leading implants for the treatment of long and small bone fractures. Some examples of our portfolio can be found via this link: https://www.stryker.com/us/en/portfolios/orthopaedics/trauma-and-extremities.html
   

  

 Reporting to the Business Unit Manager of Trauma Northern Europe, you will be part of a high caliber, achievement-driven sales team who shares a strong belief in helping our customers to improve patient care through the use of our products. You will lead your team with full accountability for sales performance, turnover and cost control, ensuring excellent customer service while driving the acquisition of new customers. For this position, you
 **reside either in Sweden, Norway or Denmark** 
 .
   

  

 This is a great opportunity to join one of the world’s leading medical technology organizations and make a difference towards our mission of making healthcare better!
   

  

**What you will do:**
* In your new role, you will start with a familiarization phase to learn about our portfolio and build relationships with internal/external stakeholders.
* Lead, develop and inspire a sales team of ~7 direct reports: you will coach/develop your team to achieve their full potential and promote an environment that fosters engagement and individual growth. Here, you will also act as a developer of people, creating a future pipeline of talent for Stryker.
* Support and drive your team strategically and tactically to accelerate business through the Trauma portfolio, in alignment with the annual marketing plan.
* You will deliver/exceed against the quarterly and yearly business budget whilst managing equipment utilization, recurring revenue pipeline development alongside accurate forecasting vs. budget.
* Build up a network with key customers, decision-makers, and key opinion leaders (KOLs).
* In close collaboration with Marketing, you will understand and utilize market dynamics to support business acceleration, taking a creative approach to new business development for both current and future technologies.
* Provide solutions for customers and KOLs, adding value to the Sales process – resulting in winning business and positive customer experience.
* Closely support and collaborate with enabling function teams such as tender & pricing, compliance, customer service and finance, to ensure smooth order to cash process is maintained and our customers receive the highest levels of service.
* Collaborate with Sales Managers of other divisions of Stryker to foster cross-departmental opportunities.


**What you need:**
 Required:
   

  

* Degree or equivalent.
* 8+ years of experience, including Sales and/or Marketing experience in industries such as MedTech, Biotech, Pharma or related.
* Excellent communication, negotiation and presentation skills with proficiency in spoken and written English.
* You have already proven your leadership skills in successfully managing or coaching a team.
* Business acumen in setting sales coverage, leveraging territory coverage and customer focus. You have experience in delivering market share growth and financial commitments.
* Ability to build relationships, excellent collaboration and cross-functional influencing skills & teamwork – your personality also makes it easy for you to inspire, motivate and develop others.
* Adaptability, analytical and organizational skills with a strategic approach to business planning.
* Demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word & PowerPoint).
* As this position involves customer interactions in the field, you have a valid driver’s license and flexibility to travel in the region.
* Ethics and integrity in ensuring the delivery of Stryker’s mission of making healthcare better.


 Preferred:
   

  

* Orthopaedics/anatomy knowledge is not mandatory, but would be an advantage.


**What we offer:**
* Flat hierarchies and a culture committed to achieve our mission and deliver remarkable results.
* We grow talent: we offer great personal and internal career development for our employees, with the possibility to make both vertical and lateral position movement.
* An attractive salary and benefits package, including a pension plan.
* Recognition of your performance: at Stryker, outstanding individual and cross-functional team performance is rewarded. Ideas, projects, or other exceptional achievements can be submitted for various awards on a local and global level.
* Collaborative and inclusive environment: join a team that values diversity and inclusion, fostering a supportive and innovative workplace.
* Quality products that improve the lives of our customers and patients.
* Ability to discover your strengths, follow your passion and own your career.


 Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies and, together with its customers, is driven to make healthcare better. The company offers innovative products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology, Orthopaedics and Spine that help improve patient and healthcare outcomes. Alongside its customers around the world, Stryker impacts more than 150 million patients annually.

title: HP Growth Manager (temporary)
description:
Take charge of growing the HP business in Sweden’s commercial and public segment, driving strategic initiatives, building strong relationships with HP and Dustin account managers, and making a tangible impact on business development success.
 **Note: This is a temporary contract for 12-months, with the possibility of extension.**
**This is what makes the position as HP Growth Manager unique**
* Drive growth of HP's Client Portfolio business by building strong, strategic relationships
* Identify new business opportunities using HP data, tools, and leads to actively support commercial sales growth
* Take ownership of driving HP business growth and creating impactful initiatives that deliver measurable results


 In this role, you will be responsible for driving the growth of the Client Portfolio business and strengthening relationships with our strategic vendor, HP. You will be part of the vendor management team while working closely with the commercial and public sales team to develop impactful initiatives that stimulate business growth. Your responsibilities will include building strong connections between HP and Dustin account managers, identifying new business opportunities, and leveraging HP’s tools, data, and leads to support commercial sales.
   

  

 This hybrid role combines two days at our Nacka office with one day at HP’s office, plus flexibility for remote work. You’ll support larger deals, join HP events, and work closely with the vendor manager driving the overall strategy, while you focus on business development.
   

  

 My name is Arjan Kats. I'm the hiring manager for the HP growth Manager role. As a leader, I value personal development and guidance highly. We place a strong emphasis on personal development and guidance, fully tailored to each employee’s expertise and needs, while remaining mindful of the different tasks they undertake. You may be highly autonomous in one area yet require additional support in another; we will address these areas together, empowering you to grow both professionally and personally. Furthermore, there is always room for your own contribution, as we believe your ideas can drive success.
   

  

**Your role & influence as HP Growth Manager**
*Responsibility* 
 : You will be given responsibility for significantly growing HP’s Client Portfolio business within the commercial and public segment in Sweden, driving strategic initiatives and business development together with both HP and Dustin account managers—essentially acting in a Business Development Manager capacity
   

  

*Collaboration* 
 : Together with your team, you take care of strengthening the relationship with our strategic vendors within Dustin. You'll work closely not only with your immediate team members but also with departments such as Sales, Category Management, and Marketing, aligning on strategic business plans and ensuring the best possible communication.
   

  

**Core Activities**
* Grow HP’s Client Portfolio business in the commercial and public segment
* Act as the bridge between Dustin and HP account managers
* Work closely with the vendor manager to develop and launch impactful initiatives


**Benefits Of Joining Dustin**
 We believe there's more to Dustin than just the benefits listed here. You'll fully understand this once you're with us. Curious? Our Life at Dustin page has you covered.
   

  

**You can find more of our benefits below in the vacancy.**
**This Is What We Imagine You Bring**
 We envision you as a curious and driven individual who is passionate about business development, building strong relationships, and driving significant growth in strategic partnerships.
 *Please note: we are not looking for consultants, freelance professionals, or acquisition based on this vacancy.*
**Additionally, It's Important That**
* Experience with business development or commercial sales
* Strong presentation skills – expected to speak to groups regularly
* Familiar with relationship building and nurturing
* You gain a lot of energy from actively wanting to drive the business forward
* Fluent in English and Swedish, with English being the primary language used across the company


**What can you expect next?**
 To Ensure a Fair Recruitment Process Free From Unconscious Bias, We Handle Your Application Anonymously And Do Not Accept Cover Letters. Please Refrain From Sending One, So That The Selection Is Based Solely On Professional Competencies. We Expect The Following Steps In The Recruitment Process
   

  

* Telephone screening with TA partner Elza van Grootel-Coenraats
* 1st interview with the Hiring Manager Arjan Kats
* Assessment (Personality- and cognitive thinking test)
* 2nd interview with the Vendormanager HP & Arjan Kats


 For questions about the position, please contact the TA Partner Elza via e-mail.
   

  

* How to Become a Dustineer
   

  

  

  

	+ Life at Dustin
	   
	
	  
	
	  
	
	  
	
		- Our products & services
		   
		
		  
		
		  
		
		  
		
		 Dustin is a leading online based IT partner in the Nordics and the Benelux. We help our customers to stay in the forefront by providing them with the right IT solution for their needs.
		   
		
		  
		
		 We offer approximately 280,000 products with related services to companies, the public sector and private individuals. Sales for the financial year 2023/24 amounted to approximately SEK 21.5 billion and more than 90 per cent of the revenues came from the corporate market.
		   
		
		  
		
		 Dustin has approximately 2,000 employees and has been listed on Nasdaq Stockholm since 2015 with headquarters in Nacka Strand just outside central Stockholm.

title: Sales Manager - Stockholm, Sweden
description:
**About FCM** 




 FCM is more than a global travel management company with 24/7 support in 100+ countries. We’re a movement—6,500+ changemakers, travel experts, and performance drivers redefining corporate travel. We don’t just keep the world’s biggest companies moving—we make it smoother, smarter, and bolder. Certified as a great place to work and fuelled by an alternative mindset, we embrace change, challenge convention, and do things differently. This is FCM—where different happens.
 



**About The Role** 




 As a Sales Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in accelerating FCM’s growth in one of our fastest‑growing markets. You’ll own the full sales lifecycle, from identifying and engaging new prospects through to closing profitable, long‑term partnerships with Finnish small to mid‑sized organisations.
 



 Working closely with a Nordic and EMEA sales team, you’ll engage senior decision‑makers, deliver compelling value‑led proposals, and demonstrate how FCM’s market‑leading technology and service model help businesses perform better. While your primary focus will be Finland, this role offers future opportunities to support growth across the wider Nordic region.
 



 You’ll also be responsible for building a strong pipeline, increasing FCM’s brand presence in the Finnish market, and contributing valuable local market insight to regional sales strategy.
 



**About You** 




 You’re an experienced B2B sales professional with a strong track record of winning complex, multi‑stakeholder deals in the Finnish market. You understand local business culture, procurement processes, and how to influence senior stakeholders with confidence and credibility.
 



 You bring:
 


* 4+ years’ experience in B2B sales, including success in closing mid‑market or enterprise‑level contracts
* Proven ability to manage the full sales cycle, from prospecting to negotiation and close
* Strong presentation, communication, and relationship‑building skills
* An existing Finnish business network and the ability to secure senior‑level meetings
* Fluency in Swedish and English
* Experience using CRM tools such as Salesforce and a structured, data‑driven approach to pipeline management
* A proactive, self‑driven mindset with the ambition to grow both personally and commercially



 If you’re motivated by growth, autonomy, and the opportunity to shape FCM’s presence in Finland, this is a chance to make a real impact.
 



 Our number one philosophy is Our people. Flight Centre Travel Group's promise is to provide an environment with equality of respect, dignity and opportunity for all our employees. We value an inclusive and supportive workplace which reflects the diversity of our society.
 



 We welcome accommodation requests to help make our hiring and onboarding experience as accessible as possible. Please advise us about accommodation needs at any point by contacting our Recruitment Team at RecruitmentUK@flightcentre.co.uk .
 



 #FCM

title: Administrativ assistent, vikariat - Socialförvaltningen
description:
**Välkommen till oss**
 Vi söker nu en vikarierande administrativ assistent till enheten Jour- och familjehem Stockholms stad.
   

  

 Kom och bli en del av vår verksamhet! Då en av våra administrativa assistenter planerar tjänstledighet söker vi nu en vikarie för denne.
   

  

**Vi erbjuder**
 Vi erbjuder en utvecklande och stimulerande roll i en föränderlig och växande verksamhet, där den ena dagen inte är den andra lik. Du introduceras i arbetet genom att jobba nära en trevlig arbetsgrupp bestående av familjehemsinspektörer och gruppledare. Du är även en del av det administrativa teamet på enheten.
   

  

 Vi erbjuder dig även:
   

  

* friskvårdsbidrag
* flexibel arbetstid
* vinter- och sommararbetstid
* möjlighet till semesterväxling


 Läs mer om dina förmåner som anställd i Stockholms stad: Förmåner för medarbetare - Stockholms stad (jobba.stockholm)
   

  

 Din blivande arbetsplats är belägen i Farsta centrum, nära tunnelbanan.
   

  

**Din roll**
 Du kommer självständigt ansvara för administrationen för en av arbetsgrupperna, externa jourhem, och ingår i ett enhetsövergripande administrativt team med ytterligare tre administrativa assistenter som sträcker sig över hela området. Teamet träffas för möten en gång i månaden och har delvis gemensamma beröringspunkter. Du har närmsta arbetsledning från gruppledare och rapporterar till enhetschef.
   

  

 Arbetet som administrativ assistent är en mångfacetterad tjänst med många olika arbetsuppgifter. Du är ansiktet utåt i olika sammanhang vilket medför kontakt med många personer, såväl internt inom staden som externt.
   

  

 Rollen är ett perfekt för dig som tycker om administration, samverkan och samordning i en ständigt föränderlig miljö.
   

  

**Vanligt förekommande arbetsuppgifter är:**
* Hantera inkommande underlag som rör arbetet inom enheten
* Följa arbetet som sker i arbetsgruppen och bistå med administrativt stöd så som registrering i Paraplyet, registrering i gemensamma listor, rekvirera och administrera registerutdrag från olika myndigheter, kommunicera beslutsprotokoll från Individutskottet, bevaka olika typer av underlag och datum samt arkivering.
* Hantering av inköp och fakturor i stadens ekonomisystem Agresso
* Stötta cheferna med administration av nyanställningar, avtackningar och gemensamma träffar
* Årlig inventering och arkivering
* sedvanliga administrativa arbetsuppgifter


 Det ligger ett mycket stort fokus på registerutdrag, en arbetsuppgift som är dominerande för denna tjänst. Arbetet med registerutdrag är mycket viktigt för familjehemsutredningen och den kontroll som enheten genomför. Det innebär att ett stort ansvar ligger på denna tjänst att administrera ett stort antal registerutdrag dagligen. Arbetet kräver en hög förmåga till självständigt arbete.
   

  

**Din kompetens och erfarenhet**
**För denna tjänst krävs att du har:**
* minst gymnasieexamen, gärna med ekonomiinriktning eller annan utbildning som arbetsgivaren bedömer som likvärdig
* minst tre års aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet som administratör/administrativ assistent inom myndighet eller offentlig verksamhet
* aktuell erfarenhet av arbete som administrativ assistent inom myndighetsutövande socialtjänst, barn och ungdom.
* erfarenhet av att samverka över gränserna mellan verksamheter
* mycket goda dokumenterade IT kunskaper och vana att arbeta med Officepaketets olika program, i synnerhet Excel
* erfarenhet av ekonomisystem och faktureringsarbete
* goda vitsord i administrativ förmåga och hög kapacitet att strukturera ditt arbete självständigt
* god kommunikativ förmåga i svenska, både i tal och skrift


**Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av:**
* arbete inom Stockholms stad.
* sociala system, Paraplyet
* administrativt arbete familjehemsvård


 Du har god förmåga att planera och prioritera ditt arbete på ett strukturerat sätt. Att ta instruktioner är lätt för dig och du tycker om att ta ansvar och driva ditt eget arbete framåt. Du anpassar dig enkelt till förändrade omständigheter och ser möjligheter i detta.
   

  

 Då arbetet innebär många kontakter såväl internt som externt behöver du vara samarbetsinriktad och ha lätt för att bygga goda relationer. Att vara serviceinriktad kommer naturligt för dig och du har intresse, vilja och förmåga till att hjälpa andra, där du gör ditt yttersta för att leverera lösningar. I rollen är det viktigt att handla i enlighet med fattade beslut, verksamhetsplan, mål, policys och riktlinjer och att vara medveten om mål och kvalitetsstandard.
   

  

**Övrigt**
 Område jour- och familjehem samt korttidsvistelse består av fyra enheter. Av dessa enheter har en fokus på rekrytering, utbildning och förmedling av jour- och familjehem och en enhet har som fokus stöd och handledning till samma målgrupp samt till adoptivfamiljer.
   

  

 Stor vikt kommer att läggas vid personlig lämplighet, motivation och engagemang för tjänsten.
   

  

 Urvalstester kan komma att ingå som en del av rekryteringsprocessen. Du blir informerad i samband med eventuell intervju.
   

  

 Intervjuer kan komma att ske innan ansökningstiden gått ut.
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan!

title: Senior Delivery Consultant - Machine Learning (GenAI), Public Sector
description:
**Description**
 The Amazon Web Services Professional Services (ProServe) team is seeking a skilled Delivery Consultant to join our team at Amazon Web Services (AWS). In this role, you'll work closely with customers to design, implement, and manage AWS solutions that meet their technical requirements and business objectives. You'll be a key player in driving customer success through their cloud journey, providing technical expertise and best practices throughout the project lifecycle.
   

  

 Possessing a deep understanding of AWS products and services, as a Delivery Consultant you will be proficient in architecting complex, scalable, and secure solutions tailored to meet the specific needs of each customer. You’ll work closely with stakeholders to gather requirements, assess current infrastructure, and propose effective migration strategies to AWS. As trusted advisors to our customers, providing guidance on industry trends, emerging technologies, and innovative solutions, you will be responsible for leading the implementation process, ensuring adherence to best practices, optimizing performance, and managing risks throughout the project.
   

  

* This role requires you to be a Swedish National and have business level proficiency in the Swedish language


 Key job responsibilities
   

  

 Design and implement complex, scalable, and secure AWS solutions tailored to customer needs.
   

  

 Collaborate with stakeholders to gather requirements and propose effective migration strategies.
   

  

 Providing technical guidance and troubleshooting support throughout project delivery.
   

  

 Act as a trusted advisor to customers on industry trends and emerging technologies.
   

  

 Sharing knowledge within the organization through mentoring, training, and creating reusable artifacts.
   

  

 A day in the life
   

  

 The AWS Professional Services organization is a global team of experts that help customers realize their desired business outcomes when using the AWS Cloud. We work together with customer teams and the AWS Partner Network (APN) to execute enterprise cloud computing initiatives. Our team provides assistance through a collection of offerings which help customers achieve specific outcomes related to enterprise cloud adoption. We also deliver focused guidance through our global specialty practices, which cover a variety of solutions, technologies, and industries.
   

  

**About The Team**
 Diverse Experiences
   

  

 Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.
   

  

 Why AWS
   

  

 Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.
   

  

 Work/Life Balance
   

  

 We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.
   

  

 Inclusive Team Culture
   

  

 AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion events foster stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.
   

  

 Mentorship and Career Growth
   

  

 We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing
   

  

**Basic Qualifications**
* 5+ years of AI and ML experience and data querying languages (e.g. SQL), scripting languages (e.g. Python) or statistical/mathematical software (e.g. R, SAS, Matlab, etc.)
* 5+ years of cloud architecture and implementation experience . Proven track record in designing and developing end-to-end Machine Learning and Generative AI solutions, from conception to deployment.
* Experience in applying best practices and evaluating alternative and complementary ML and foundational models suitable for given business contexts.
* Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as statistics, mathematics, data science, business analytics, economics, finance, engineering, or computer science or equivalent experience. Knowledge of data modeling principles, statistical analysis methodologies, and demonstrated ability to extract meaningful insights from complex, large-scale datasets.


**Preferred Qualifications**
* AWS experience preferred, with proficiency in a wide range of AWS services (e.g., Bedrock, SageMaker, EC2, S3, Lambda, IAM, VPC, CloudFormation) AWS Associate level certifications (e.g., Machine Learning Specialty, Machine Learning Engineer Associate, Solutions Architect Professional) preferred.
* Experience with automation and scripting (e.g., Terraform, Python). Knowledge of security and compliance standards (e.g., HIPAA, GDPR). Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to both technical and non-technical audiences.
* Experience in developing and optimizing foundation models (LLMs), including fine-tuning, continuous training,and implementation of AI systems.


 Amazon is an equal opportunities employer. We believe passionately that employing a diverse workforce is central to our success. We make recruiting decisions based on your experience and skills. We value your passion to discover, invent, simplify and build. Protecting your privacy and the security of your data is a longstanding top priority for Amazon. Please consult our Privacy Notice (https://www.amazon.jobs/en/privacy\_page) to know more about how we collect, use and transfer the personal data of our candidates.
   

  

 Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
   

  

  

**Company** 
 - AWS EMEA SARL (Sweden Branch) - G54
   

  

 Job ID: A10395877

title: Senior Data Asset Owner
description:
**Job ID:4225**
 We are looking for a dedicated person who will be responsible for establishing and maintaining governance on our key data assets. This is an opportunity for you to become a key player in building Nordea’s information/data foundation for critical data areas – work that enables regulatory compliance and supports seamless customer experiences.
   

  

 Compliance and integrity are not just requirements – they are a competitive advantage. It is about ensuring we remain a trusted partner to our customers and society.Together, we are passionate about building a bank that makes a difference – one where you can learn and grow. Will you help us lead the way in embedding compliance to transform the future of banking?
   

  

**About Our Team**
 You will join the Data Management unit in PeB COO, where we understand and govern PeB’s critical data assets. We continuously strive to improve the understanding and quality of our data.
   

  

 Collaboration. Ownership. Passion. Courage. These are the values that guide us in how we work and how we make decisions – and that we imagine you share with us.
   

  

**What You’ll Be Doing**
 As the Data Asset Owner in PeB you are accountable for bringing and maintaining your data asset(s) under governance.
   

  

**This includes:**
* Ensure adherence to Nordea business and Nordea regulatory requirements
* Facilitate dialogue and engage stakeholders to define Data Governance implementation scope
* Define, document and implement data asset-specific data standards to ensure business understanding
* Document and implement data quality standards in accordance with Nordea data standards and data consumer requirements
* Ensure that data quality measurements are performed, and that data quality assurance processes and procedures are implemented


 This position will be located in Stockholm.
   

  

**Who You Are**
 This is the right role for you if you have a strong interest towards data and technology, enjoy building relationships with diverse teams across the organization, and are curious about the critical role data plays in regulatory compliance and business optimization.
   

  

**Your background and skills include:**
* Familiarity with Data Governance and Data Quality Management in Nordea
* Strong communication and stakeholder management skills
* Self-motivated with a strong sense of ownership for your deliverables
* Fluency in English


**It would be ideal if you also:**
* Have a solid understanding of the financial industry
* Are proficient in any of the Nordic languages


**What We Offer**
 People come here when they want to get somewhere. For some, it is to take their career to the next level. For others, it is to break new ground within their area of expertise – in other words, with us, you will always move forward.
   

  

 A culture that fosters performance and growth in one of the largest Nordic banks, offering various opportunities to evolve, develop and learn from brilliant colleagues with diverse backgrounds in a vibrant working environment.
   

  

 Hybrid working model – we believe in the value of bringing people together and at the same time we embrace the freedom of flexibility.
   

  

 Diversity and inclusion are a natural part of our daily work. We know that an inclusive workplace is a sustainable one. We genuinely believe that our diverse backgrounds, experiences, characteristics and traits make us stronger together. Every day we strive to find new ways to improve diversity and inclusion within our community e.g. we have signed the European Diversity Charters in the countries where we operate to show our commitment and engage with others to continue learning and improving.
   

  

 If this sounds like you, get in touch!
   

  

**Next steps:**
 Submit your application no later than 15/05/2026. For more information, you’re welcome to contact Hanna Petersons at hanna.petersons@nordea.com.
   

  

**For candidates in Sweden:** 
 For union information, please contact finansforbundet@nordea.se or SACONordea@nordea.com.

title: Upphandlare med ansvar för samordning och arbete mot välfärdsbrott till Skarpnäcks stadsdelsförvaltning
description:
Vill du kombinera operativt upphandlingsarbete med ett viktigt samhällsuppdrag? Vi söker en samordnande upphandlare som vill bidra till en rättssäker och effektiv användning av offentliga medel och samtidigt vara med och utveckla arbetet mot välfärdsbrott. Är du upphandlare idag och vill ta nästa steg där du får bredda ditt uppdrag kan detta vara rätt roll för dig!
 



  





**Välkommen till oss** 




 Som samordnande upphandlare utgår du från kontoret i Johanneshov och tillhör enheten för ekonomi och internservice. Du välkomnas till en enhet bestående av tio medarbetare och enhetschef som utgör förvaltningens verksamhetsstöd inom budgetarbete och uppföljning, controlling, lokalplanering, upphandling, it- och telefoni samt vaktmästeri. Du blir en del av ett kompetent och utvecklingsinriktat team där vi har ett tätt samarbete och delar med oss av vår erfarenhet. Hos oss finns goda möjligheter för dig att bidra med dina perspektiv och vara med och utveckla verksamheten.
 



  





 Rollen som upphandlare med ansvar för samordning och arbete mot välfärdsbrott är ny. Det innebär att du tillsammans med enhetschef och kollegor, får vara med och forma uppdraget och arbetssätten framåt. Här får du goda möjligheter att påverka och sätta din prägel på hur vi arbetar inom området.
 



  





**Hälsning från din framtida chef** 




 Hej! Jag heter Mattias och kommer att vara din närmaste chef. Jag har bred erfarenhet av kommunal verksamhet och ett särskilt intresse för upphandlings- och inköpsfrågor. Mitt ledarskap präglas av engagemang och tillit och jag drivs av att tillsammans utveckla verksamheten och skapa värde för våra invånare. Varmt välkommen till oss!
 



  





**Vi erbjuder** 



* Förskottssemester, vilket innebär betalda semesterdagar redan första anställningsåret utifrån när på året du anställs.
* Möjlighet till fler semesterdagar genom semesterväxling
* Kortare arbetstid under sommarmånaderna
* Friskvårdsbidrag och förmånliga priser på stadens egna sim- och träningsanläggningar
* En flexibel arbetsvardag med möjlighet att arbeta hemifrån när arbetsuppgifterna tillåter
* Läs gärna mer om Stockholms stads förmåner här



  





**Din roll** 




 Som samordnande upphandlare har du en delad roll där du kombinerar operativt inköps- och upphandlingsarbete samt avtalsuppföljning med ett mer strategiskt och utvecklingsinriktat ansvar. Tyngdpunkten i din roll ligger i att genomföra upphandlingar, hantera avrop, följa upp avtal och stötta verksamheten i deras inköps- och upphandlingsarbete, medan en mindre del av uppdraget handlar om att driva och samordna arbetet med att förebygga otillåten påverkan och välfärdsbrott.
 



  





 Genom ditt arbete bidrar du till att upptäcka, förebygga och motverka kriminalitet i välfärden. Du planerar och samordnar stadsdelsförvaltningens arbete mot välfärdsbrott samt ingår i nätverk tillsammans med stadens centrala funktioner för att stärka det gemensamma arbetet inom området.
 



  




* Vara ett stöd till förvaltningens verksamheter i frågor som rör inköp, upphandling och användning av avtal, inklusive rådgivning och internutbildning kring upphandlingsregler och aktuella ramavtal
* Hantera förvaltningens delar i stadens inköps- och upphandlingssystem, samt stödja verksamheterna i användningen av avtalsdatabaser
* Ansvara för uppföljning och efterlevnad av aktuella avtal
* Planera, samordna och utveckla förvaltningens arbete med att förebygga otillåten påverkan och välfärdsbrott, inklusive utbildningsinsatser och kunskapsspridning
* Delta i stadens relevanta nätverk och samverka externt samt ansvara för omvärldsbevakning inom området



  





**Din kompetens och erfarenhet** 




 Vi söker dig som har
 


* En eftergymnasial utbildning inom ekonomi, juridik, upphandling/inköp eller annat område som arbetsgivaren bedömer motsvarande
* Några års aktuell arbetslivserfarenhet inom upphandling, till exempel som upphandlare, inköpare eller motsvarande
* God kunskap och erfarenhet inom LOU
* God systemvana i upphandlings- och avtalssystem
* Mycket goda kunskaper i svenska i såväl tal som skrift



  





 Det är meriterande om du även tar med dig
 


* Erfarenhet av frågor som rör välfärdsbrott, nätverkskriminalitet eller annan organiserad brottslighet
* Erfarenhet från arbete inom eller med en politisk styrd organisation
* Systemerfarenhet i Agresso och/eller Kommers



  





 Som person är du självständig och trivs med att ta ansvar för och driva dina frågor framåt. Du är strukturerad och har förmåga att bygga upp arbetssätt, rutiner och strukturer från grunden. Vi tror att du trivs i en roll där du får kombinera samordning med eget ansvar, där du fungerar som en sammanhållande länk mellan olika delar av verksamheten samtidigt som du känner dig trygg i att hantera egna uppdrag. Du är kommunikativ och har ett pedagogiskt och engagerande sätt att uttrycka dig. Det gör att du har lätt för att informera, skapa förståelse och bidra till utveckling inom dina ansvarsområden.
 



  





**Övrigt** 




 Vi rekryterar utan det personliga brevet i denna rekrytering för att stärka fokus på de kompetenser och erfarenheter som efterfrågas för denna roll. Vid bedömningen av din ansökan kommer vi endast att utgå ifrån ditt CV och ett antal frågor du får besvara i samband med att du söker tjänsten.
 



  





 Vi önskar att du svarar konkret och kortfattat på frågorna, och att det finns en tydlig koppling mellan dina svar och ditt CV
 



  





 Tjänsten omfattas av registerkontroll och vi kommer begära utdrag från belastningsregistret innan överenskommelse om anställning fattas.

title: Visual Merchandiser
description:
**Jobbeskrivning**

 As a Visual Merchandiser, you play a pivotal role in shaping an exceptional in-store customer journey. With a keen eye for aesthetics and a strong commercial mindset, you bring the brand’s vision to life—elevating the collection in a way that is both inspiring and aligned with business goals. Your work not only enhances the visual appeal of the store but also drives customer engagement and sales performance.
   

  





**Key Responsibilities:**



* Delivering an excellent customer experience through expert styling advice, deep product knowledge, and a visually compelling store environment
* Interpreting sales data and customer insights to inform and execute the store’s visual direction
* Designing and installing impactful window displays that reflect seasonal trends and brand identity
* Styling mannequins and creating outfit combinations that inspire and influence customer purchasing decisions
* Merchandising the shop floor in alignment with company guidelines to ensure a cohesive and commercial presentation
* Supporting the sales team in daily store operations and contributing to a seamless customer experience
* Setting and maintaining high visual merchandising standards, while training and mentoring team members to uphold consistency
* Acting as a brand ambassador, ensuring every visual element contributes to a memorable and engaging customer journey



  

  

  

  




**Kvalifikationer**
* Customer-Centric Mindset: Understanding of how to elevate in-store experiences through impactful visual storytelling and exceptional customer service.
* Visual Merchandising Standards: Skilled in executing brand visual guidelines, including window displays, mannequin styling, and in-store presentation to enhance commercial impact.
* Fashion Knowledge: Strong understanding of current fashion trends, silhouettes, and seasonal styling, with a passion for translating them into compelling visual displays.
* Commercial Awareness: Some experience in using KPIs, sales data, and customer feedback to inform visual merchandising decisions and drive store performance.
* Communication You have a genuine interest in people, communicate clearly, and actively listen to understand their perspectives and needs.
* Adaptability & Flexibility: Adaptable and responsive to changing priorities and customer requests.
* Eager to learn: Open to continuous learning and actively seeks opportunities for personal and professional growth.
* Comfortable working varied hours, including evenings, weekends, and holidays.
* English language skills



  

  





  

  

  

  




**Ytterligare information**
* This is a
 **permanent** 
**full-time** 
**position with a contract of 38,25** 
**hours a week** 
 . Start date as soon as possible.



**Benefits:**




 We’re committed to supporting our employees with a range of attractive benefits and valuable development opportunities. As a member of our team, you’ll enjoy a 25% staff discount across all H&M Group brands—available both in-store and online. Additionally, every employee is included in our H&M Incentive Program (HIP), designed to reward your contributions and support your growth within the company. You can read more about our H&M Incentive Program here.
   

  





 In addition to this, Sweden-based colleagues also receive:
   

  




* Long Service Award
* Collective agreement
* Wellness Allowance



**Inclusion & Diversity** 
  

  





 At H&M Group, we are committed to fostering inclusive, diverse, and equitable workplaces across our entire organisation. We believe that teams thrive when they bring together a wide range of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. This diversity enhances our ability to solve problems creatively, expand our thinking, and build meaningful connections with colleagues and customers around the world. That’s why we consider all dimensions of diversity throughout our recruitment process—ensuring our workforce reflects the richness of the communities we serve.
   

  





**Company Description** 
  

  

  

  

 Weekday is where culture moves fast and expression runs deep. Your everyday remix wrapped in denim. Too much, not enough, perfect. A wardrobe of contrasts: Effortless basics, monochrome shades, and edgy cuts. The signature jeans that go with everything—and express it all. More than fashion - it’s a moment, a mood, culture in motion. Every version, all at once.

title: Senior Software Engineer (Java + Observability) - Remote Work
description:
At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.
 



  





 Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.
 



  





 When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.
 



  





 Senior Software Engineer (Java + Observability) at BairesDev
 



  





 We’re looking for a Senior Software Engineer with Java skills and real experience building observability and telemetry infrastructure. You’ve worked on metrics pipelines, instrumentation frameworks, or monitoring systems for large-scale distributed environments — and you understand the difference between using these tools and actually building them. This is a platform engineering role: you’ll be creating the tooling that makes complex distributed systems observable for engineering teams, not just operating them.
 



  





 What You’ll Do:
 



  





 - Design and develop Java-based observability and telemetry platform components for distributed systems.
 



 - Build and maintain metrics pipelines, tracing frameworks, and instrumentation tooling.
 



 - Integrate observability standards such as OpenTelemetry into platform infrastructure.
 



 - Work with engineering teams to understand observability requirements and deliver effective tooling.
 



 - Deploy and maintain observability platform services in Kubernetes environments.
 



 - Contribute to code reviews and engineering standards across the platform team.
 



  





 What We Are Looking For:
 



  





 - 5+ years of experience in IT roles.
 



 - 3+ years of experience with Java as a primary programming language.
 



 - Hands-on experience building or working with observability tooling including OpenTelemetry, metrics systems, or tracing.
 



 - Solid understanding of distributed systems and the challenges of making them observable at scale.
 



 - Experience working in Kubernetes environments.
 



 - Advanced proficiency in English.
 



  





 How we do make your work (and your life) easier:
 



  





 - Remote Work.
 



 - Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred
 



 - Hardware and software setup for you to work from home.
 



 - Flexible hours: create your own schedule.
 



 - Paid parental leaves, vacations, and national holidays.
 



 - Innovative and multicultural work environment: collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent.
 



 - Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities.
 



  





 Join a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive and make a significant impact!
 



  





 Apply now!

title: GitHub Solutions Architect
description:
**Welcome to Eficode!**
 We are building the future of software development - specialising in AI, Security, Cloud, DevOps, Agile, and Enterprise Service Management. We’re passionate about sharing our knowledge, not just within our teams, but with the community around us as well. We invest heavily in our people, whether through conferences, training, or other growth opportunities. We believe that with the right tools and mindset, anything is possible.
   

  

**About The Role**
 As our GitHub Solutions Architect, you will be driving the adoption of enterprise-grade GitHub platforms. You will own the end-to-end design and implementation of secure, scalable development workflows for some of the most influential organizations in the Nordics. You will act as an advisor, bridging the gap between high-level business requirements and hands-on technical excellence.
   

  

**What You’ll Accomplish**
* Architect the future: Design enterprise-grade GitHub architectures that align with complex business, security, and regulatory requirements
* Lead global transformations: Guide customers through complex transitions from platforms like Azure DevOps, GitLab, or Bitbucket.
* Technical mentorship: Elevating the standards of our GitHub practice by mentoring consultants and sharing knowledge internally.
* Strategic advisory: Conducting maturity assessments and defining adoption roadmaps for executive and technical stakeholders.
* Advancing security: Implementing DevSecOps principles and advanced security controls, including GitHub Advanced Security (GHAS).


**Who We Are Looking For**
 To thrive in this role, you need more than deep engineering roots: you also need a business-first mindset and a consultative heart.
   

  

**Consultative and business mindset**
* Strategic advisory: You can translate business needs into sustainable technical solutions.
* Commercial awareness: You look beyond delivery to identify growth opportunities and help shape scalable consulting offerings.
* Pre-sales fluency: You are comfortable working with the sales team and leading technical conversations to win client trust.
* Technical mentorship: You enjoy elevating others by sharing knowledge and defining technical standards.


**Core Technical Requirements**
* DevOps engineering: Solid experience in modern software delivery, with a deep understanding of Infrastructure as Code (IaC), cloud-native architecture, and quality gates like SonarQube.
* GitHub proficiency in designing GitHub architectures and version control strategies, alongside sophisticated CI/CD automation, GHAS implementation and provide technical advisory on AI-assisted coding best practices


 Please note this position is based in Sweden (Stockholm, Malmö or Gothenburg) and requires you to be located in Sweden and have a valid work permit.
   

  

**Why Eficode?**
* Join a GitHub powerhouse: We have a strong partnership with GitHub.
* Have a real impact: Your work directly shapes how major organizations build software, providing them with peace of mind and a competitive edge.
* Contribute to a community of bright minds on all SDLC, DevOps and AI topics.
* Enjoy flexibility and freedom: We offer hybrid work opportunities. While we have a vibrant culture with social events at the office, you have the freedom to shape your work-life balance.
* Invest in your growth: Beyond our Efi Academy, we prioritize continuous learning and support you in your career development.


 To learn more about working at Eficode, benefits and recruitment process, check our career website.
   

  

**Ready to build with us?**
 This position is open to candidates across Scandinavia, the UK and Poland. If you're looking forward to building and leading your team to success, we encourage you to apply by submitting your CV, highlighting the projects and the industries you’ve worked on.
   

  

 We're looking forward to reading you!
   

  

**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Eficode we are committed to offering an inclusive recruitment process and equal opportunities for all applicants.
   

  

*We are dedicated to creating a work environment that embraces diversity, inclusion, and respect for all employees. We believe that our differences make us better. We recognize that by having diverse perspectives, skills, ages, genders, backgrounds and cultures we will better drive our customers forward and innovate in our field.*
*To ensure a fair recruitment process, please refrain from sharing unnecessary personal information and your picture on your job application.*
**Salary Statement**
 At Eficode, we believe your compensation should reflect the impact you make and how you grow along the way. Your salary develops through regular check-ins with your team lead, where we look at things like your skills, experience, client work, feedback, and overall contribution — both to your projects and to our culture. As a consultancy, it’s natural that factors like time spent on billable client work are part of the picture — but so are your development, collaboration, and everyday efforts that drive value. We also carry out a company-wide salary review once a year.
   

  

*On top of your salary, we offer a broad mix of benefits, both financial and non-financial, to support your well-being, work-life balance, and professional growth.*
**Find Out More About Our Benefits Here.**
**Beware of recruitment scams**
 Eficode never asks for payment, bank details, or sensitive personal information during the recruitment process. We only communicate through official Eficode email accounts and approved recruitment platforms. If you receive an unsolicited message, job offer, or request for personal or financial information claiming to be from Eficode, it may be fraudulent.

title: (Senior) Auditor – Internal Controls, SOX
description:
**Job Description**
**Who We Are**
 Verve has created a more efficient and privacy-focused way to buy and monetize advertising. Verve is an ecosystem of demand and supply technologies fusing data, media, and technology together to deliver results and growth to both advertisers and publishers–no matter the screen or location, no matter who, what, or where a customer is. With 30 offices across the globe and with an eye on servicing forward-thinking advertising customers, Verve’s solutions are trusted by more than 90 of the United States’ top 100 advertisers, 4,000 publishers globally, and the world’s top demand-side platforms. Learn more at www.verve.com
   

  

**Who You Are**
 We are seeking a highly motivated and structured (Senior) SOX Internal Controls Specialist to strengthen our growing team. This role is critical in maintaining and enhancing our internal control framework and influences the organization’s approach to finance and corporate governance. As part of a collaborative and dynamic team, you will work cross-functionally with key stakeholders across departments to identify, assess, and monitor risks, as well as support the continuous improvement of our internal control environment.
   

  

**This is a 1-year fixed-term contract (with the possibility of extending to a permanent role).**
**This is a hybrid position with three days per week in our Stockholm office.
   

  

 If you live outside Stockholm, the position can be remote in Sweden for the fixed-term period, but requires relocation in case of extending to a permanent role.**
**What You Will Do**
* Design, implement, and test internal controls under SOX/ PCAOB standards
* Assess control design and operating effectiveness, evaluate findings against the internal controls framework and financial reporting standards
* Prepare and maintain accurate audit documentation, including working papers and summary reports, and coordinate with external auditors, providing necessary documentation and timely responses to inquiries
* Partner with stakeholders across the organization (Finance, IT, Legal, HR) to align on findings and remediation actions
* Assess the impact of business process changes and new evolving risks on the control environment to identify gaps and support the implementation of processes and controls where needed
* Apply best practices to analyze and standardize processes and controls among subsidiaries
* Act proactively with a structured and collaborative approach to drive continuous improvement and foster strong working relationships across functions


**Job Requirements**
**What You Will Bring**
* Bachelor’s degree in finance, Business Administration, Accounting, or a related field
* 4+ years of experience for Mid level, and 6+ years for Senior level in external audit, internal audit, internal controls, advisory, or a combination, preferably within a complex, multinational environment
* Experience in SOX or PCAOB is mandatory (2 years minimum experience)
* Solid knowledge of IFRS or US GAAP is highly desirable
* Demonstrated experience with internal controls testing procedures and concluding on results, including documentation and communication of findings.
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with a proactive approach to collaborating, supporting the other team members, and effectively driving initiatives
* Professional certifications in progress or completed (CIA, ACCA, CISA, or equivalent) are beneficial
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (especially Excel and PowerPoint) is essential, and knowledge of Netsuite and/or Lucanet is a plus
* Please note: we can only consider candidates with the legal right to work in Sweden, as we are currently unable to provide visa sponsorship for this role


**What We Offer﻿**
 Just a few of the benefits waiting for you at Verve:
   

  

* Stay healthy and fit through our Healthcare allowance (Friskvårdsbidrag) of SEK 5000
* Support your long-term financial security with our Avanza Corporate Pension Scheme (Tjänstepension)
* Make your commute easy and affordable with our monthly SEK 580 Transportation Allowance
* Recharge with 25 paid vacation days + 3 Wellness days throughout the year, in addition to the public holidays
* Boost your professional knowledge with our annual training budget & internal webinars
* … and even more reasons to join us!


 Verve provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.

title: Finnish speaking Junior Implementation & Customer Success Specialist
description:
Skovik är en global lösning för reseräkningar och utläggshantering och används av företag som Klarna, Scandic, Lyko och Kry. Vi automatiserar tidskrävande processer, säkerställer regelefterlevnad och frigör tid för både anställda och ekonomiavdelningar i drygt 70 länder.
 



  





 Vi växer och söker en person till vårt implementations- och customer success-team.
 



  





**Rollen** 




 Mycket av din tid kommer ägnas åt att implementera Skoviks lösning, i början genom att assistera i våra projekt men du tar gradvis ett allt större självständigt ansvar. På sikt blir du arkitekten bakom kundens unika lösning: du förstår deras affärslogik, konfigurerar systemet, ger råd när integrationer byggs och skapar automatiseringar. Du blir en rådgivare till ekonomi-, löne- och IT-chefer och hjälper dem navigera skatteefterlevnad, systemintegrationer och processoptimering.
 



  





 Rollen innebär även löpande support hos kunder, både till administratörer och slutanvändare. På sikt kommer du att tilldelas egna kunder och bli deras customer success manager som proaktivt ser till att de använder lösningen på bästa sätt.
 



  





 En del av rollen handlar också om att hålla kontoret igång: praktiska inköp, koordinering med hyresvärd och redovisningsbyrå, planering av enklare event och liknande. Den här delen kan minska över tid, i takt med att teamet växer och du fokuserar helhjärtat på implementation och kundrelationer.
 



  





 Du behöver ingen förkunskap om bokföring, skatter, ekonomisystem eller programmering. Vi investerar i din utveckling.
 



  





**Framtiden** 




 Inom ett år tar du självständigt ansvar för enklare implementeringar och customer success för medelstora kunder. Med tiden finns möjlighet att ansvara för stora internationella bolag vilket kan innebära resande till storstadsregioner 1–2 gånger per år för att besöka dina kunder på plats.
 



  





**Krav** 




  





**I detta fall söker vi dig som har finska som modersmål eller minst på C2-nivå.** 
 Utöver det tror vi mer på vem du är än vad du kan lista på ditt CV. För att trivas och prestera bra i rollen behövs en kombination av skarp problemlösningsförmåga, hög ansvarskänsla och ett genuint intresse för att hjälpa.
 



  





 Konkret innebär det att du:
 


* Ser mönster i hur system och processer hänger ihop och kan lösa komplexa problem
* Trivs i professionella samtal och bygger förtroende naturligt. Dina kunder ska genuint uppskatta dig som kontaktperson.
* Är prestigelös och tar dig an arbetsuppgifter högt som lågt med samma engagemang
* Håller huvudet kallt när projekten är hektiska och levererar med fokus och lugn även under press
* Kommunicerar obehindrat på svenska, finska och engelska, i tal och skrift



  





  





 Vi ser gärna 2–3 års arbetslivserfarenhet, men lägger ingen vikt vid specifik branscherfarenhet eller utbildningsbakgrund.

title: Legal Counsel
description:
**Do you have a few years of legal experience, a genuine interest in privacy, and thrive in an environment where pace meets heart? At Akind, you won’t just be a member of the legal team – you will be a central enabler for our continued international growth.** 




  





 On behalf of
 **Akind Group** 
 , we are seeking an operational Legal Counsel to join a modern, tech-driven legal function. Positioned as a business enabler, the Legal Department combines legal expertise with information security and compliance to protect revenue and strengthen operations.
 



  





 In this role, the Legal Counsel will help safeguard company interests and provide counsel across all business functions. Reporting to the General Counsel, the successful candidate will be a key player in ensuring that legal expertise acts as a seamless driver for the business.
 



  





 This is an opportunity to be part of a transformation journey where legal operations, AI, and new ways of working are central—giving you a chance to contribute to shaping how a future-facing legal function operates.
 



  





**About the Role** 




  





 As Legal Counsel at Akind, you will step into the heart of our operations. You will be the daily backbone of our Legal Department, acting as the first point of contact for a broad range of questions across the entire business. Supporting 37 legal entities across our five brands -
 **Akind,** 
**Academic Work, Winona, Brights** 
 , and
 **Crowd Collective** 
 —in six countries, you will hold a role with immense breadth and international impact.
 



  





**Your mission will involve:** 




  




* **Commercial Contracts:** 
 Supporting and coordinating commercial contract work (drafting, reviewing, and negotiating) with a focus on speed, accuracy, and practical, solution-oriented outcomes.
* **Corporate Governance:** 
 Owning and managing corporate governance, administrative tasks, and group shareholder matters across a multi-entity group.
* **Support & Cross-Functional Collaboration:** 
 Support for the business (managing inquiries via shared inboxes) and providing pragmatic, business-relevant advice across functions such as IT, Finance, HR, and Procurement.
* **Data Protection & Compliance:** 
 Handling GDPR, data privacy, and other compliance related matters.
* **Risk Management & Problem Solving:** 
 Contributing to risk assessments and navigating new legislation.
* **Process Improvement:** 
 Supporting ongoing enhancements in ways of working, templates, guidelines, and stakeholder education to embed compliance into daily processes.



  





**Who are we?** 




  





 We are currently composed of the Akind Group General Counsel, the Head of Information and Cyber Security, a Senior Legal Counsel, and a Legal Assistant, with a blend of experience and diverse skill set and with clear ownership of critical areas.
 



  





 We are recognized for our "can-do" mentality, where trust and collective success are at the core. We support all commercial functions within the Group (including Winona, Brights, Crowd Collective and Academic Work).
 



  





 Here, you become part of a culture that lives by the values
 **Beat Yesterday, Share Energy** 
 , and
 **Show Heart** 
 . We champion autonomous teams, self-leadership, and individuals who share insights and energy to propel us forward.
 



  





**Who are you?** 




  





 We are looking for a pragmatic, business-minded legal professional who enjoys being close to the organization. You listen carefully to understand the real problem behind a question and take natural ownership of your tasks.
 



  





**We believe you have:** 




  




* A relevant academic degree in Law.
* Approximately 3 years of experience in a broad corporate legal role, ideally from a dynamic commercial environment.
* A strong interest in, or experience with, GDPR and privacy.
* Fluency in both Swedish and English.
* Experience from the tech industry, a personal-data intensive industry or an interest in information security is a significant advantage.
* Comfortable working with many stakeholders and topics in parallel, across countries and different regulatory contexts.



  





**Why Akind?** 




  





 At Akind, you don't just get a job; you join a family of brands dedicated to making people and businesses grow. You will join a modern Legal Department that is recognized as a business enabler. You will have the opportunity to build deep competence in corporate governance while contributing to a transformation journey where AI and new ways of working are central.
 



  





**Practical Information** 




  




* **Start:** 
 As soon as possible, with regard to your notice period.
* **Extent:** 
 Full-time.
* **Location:** 
 Stockholm, Linköping or Malmö.
* **Contact:** 
 In this recruitment, Akind collaborates with Winona.



  





 Our selection process is continuous, and the advert may close once we move to the interview phase—so send in your application today!
 



  





 In this process Akind is working together with Winona, if you have any questions please contact Recruitment Responsible: Sandra Isojärvi,
 
 sandra.isojarvi@winonagroup.com
 
 , or Liisa Unenge
 
 liisa.unenge@winonagroup.com
 




  





**About Akind Group** 




  





**A network designed to make people and businesses grow.** 




  





 Akind Group is a family of market-leading brands—including
 **Academic Work, Brights, Crowd Collective, and Winona** 
 —all working together to unlock the growth potential of people and companies. Headquartered in Stockholm and operating across six countries, the Group consists of over 10,000 employees driven by a mission to prepare people and businesses for the workplace of tomorrow.
 



  





 The world of work is undergoing a significant transition. Digitalization, automation, and AI are reshaping industries, and Akind Group meets this challenge head-on. By continually reinventing their processes and daring to question the status quo, the Group builds forward-thinking solutions that make the world of work better, smarter, and more efficient.
 



  





 The Group's DNA is defined by core values:
 **"Share Energy," "Beat Yesterday,"** 
 and
 **"Show Heart."** 
 These values guide the organization to embrace change, share best practices, and celebrate success together. Akind Group is not just building businesses for today; it is creating a group of companies designed to make a lasting difference for the future.

title: Senior Mechanical Design Engineer
description:
Working at CorPower Ocean you will be part of a world-class team, determined to make a big impact. We are on a mission to bring reliable and competitive wave energy to the world. We offer a high degree of autonomy and flexibility, combined with great personal responsibility. CorPower Ocean is a global leader in wave energy, with a technology that has shown game-changing performance. Your work will contribute to the global transition to renewable energy by unlocking one of the largest untapped sources of clean energy on earth.
 



  





**The Role:** 




  





 The Mechanical team at CorPower Ocean is looking for a Mechanical Design Engineer experienced in the design of large steel structures and industrial equipment to take a key role in the development and optimisation of our innovative wave energy converters.
 



 The role involves designing large, welded steel structures and mechanical systems for offshore environments, driving development from concept through testing and release for production.
 



 You will work closely with the mechanical design team but will also interact extensively with the wider organisation, including supply chain, manufacturing, testing and field installation teams, requiring strong collaboration skills.
 








**Responsibilities:** 



* Design of large mechanical structures and welded steel assemblies (from concept to release-for-production)
* Present to cross-disciplinary stakeholders during design reviews
* Perform structural analysis (hand calculations and FEA)
* Produce technical documentation for internal use and for third-party verification and certification
* Perform failure mode, effects and criticality analysis for modules and systems
* Manage your systems and components to meet performance, schedule and budget targets
* Investigate improvements to performance, reliability and manufacturability
* Interface with requirement, supply chain, manufacturing and field-testing teams
* Support assembly, testing and pilot installations in the field
* Support and mentor junior engineers when needed



  





**Skills and Experience:** 



* You have 5+ years of experience designing large mechanical systems or welded steel structures, ideally in industries such as offshore, marine, renewable energy, heavy machinery, or industrial equipment.
* You have experience designing fabricated steel structures or heavy mechanical assemblies exposed to dynamic loads, fatigue, or demanding operating environments.
* You have experience with structural analysis of large fabricated or welded steel systems, exposed to dynamic loads, fatigue, or demanding operating environments.
* Experience contributing or leading end-to-end product development cycles from design, build, test to production in multiple projects.
* You have a broad interest in mechanical systems. Experience with mooring systems, gearboxes, hydraulics, structural optimisation, pressure vessels, fabrication methods, sensors or power generation equipment is considered an advantage.
* Experience with Creo CAD, Windchill PLM and ANSYS is beneficial; equivalent tools are also relevant.
* You may have a BSc or MSc degree in mechanical engineering or a related discipline, but practical experience and engineering mindset are most important.
* You are proficient in English, both spoken and written.



  





**As a person, you:** 



* are striving for excellence, put quality into everything you do
* are self-motivated and constantly seek the most efficient ways to work
* search for simple and sustainable solutions to difficult problems
* love to learn new things, and take pride in constantly developing your skills
* are persistent, knowing it takes stamina to reach pioneering goals
* see your tasks through to completion
* are comfortable in presenting your solutions internally and externally
* are data driven and strive to always back your points with solid analysis



  





 CorPower Ocean has a diverse team with many nationalities and backgrounds, all working towards the same goal: To Power the Planet with clean energy from ocean waves.

title: Områdeschef för verksamhetsutveckling i Stockholm
description:
Placeringsort StockholmDiarienummer FK 2026/012706
   

  

 Logga in och sök jobbet Öppnas i nytt fönster
   

  

*Varje år fattar Försäkringskassan 21 miljoner beslut som påverkar människors liv och hela samhället. Vi betalar ut cirka 260 miljarder kronor varje år. Det är allas skattepengar vi har hand om. Det är ett stort ansvar. Därför sätter vi oss in i varje ärende, följer lagar och regler, informerar och vägleder, bryr oss och visar omtanke. Det är ett jobb för dig som vill vara med och ta ansvar. Välkommen till Försäkringskassan.*
 Vi söker en driven och målmedveten områdeschef till Avdelningen för funktionsnedsättning och varaktigt nedsatt arbetsförmåga. Här finns en möjlighet för dig som vill leda verksamhetsutveckling med ett samhällsviktigt uppdrag.
   

  

 Strategiskt ledarskap, utveckling, verksamhetsansvar
   

  

 Som områdeschef ansvarar du för att styra verksamhet och leda människor utifrån myndighetens långsiktiga mål och avdelningens gemensamma inriktning. Den statliga värdegrunden genomsyrar ditt arbete. Du leder verksamhetsutvecklare som arbetare brett med olika typer av utvecklingsfrågor inom området bostadstillägg, arbetsskador och arbetshjälpmedel. I ditt uppdrag är du ett stöd för verksamhetsområdets beslutsfattande och tar ansvar för att fattade beslut genomförs.
   

  

 Du skapar förståelse för sammanhang, riktning och de målbilder avdelningen satt upp och genomförandet av de planer din verksamhet har. Vidare har du verksamhetsansvar, budgetansvar samt personalansvar. Du har en aktiv roll att driva samarbetsfrågor utifrån ett större samhällsperspektiv tillsammans med andra myndigheter och aktörer. Du representerar Försäkringskassan i mediekontakter.
   

  

 Som områdeschef konkretiserar du och bryter ned mål till praktisk handling och säkerställer genom dialoger med dina medarbetare att strategier och planer förankras och genomförs. Genom ditt ledarskap skapar du den förutsägbarhet, trygghet och hållbarhet som är nödvändig för att medarbetarna ska kunna ta sitt ansvar samt möta behov av förändring. Du anpassar och utövar ditt ledarskap på ett sätt som skapar förutsättningar för medarbetarna att använda sitt omdöme och kompetens.
   

  

 Som områdeschef ingår du i avdelningens ledningsgrupp och din närmast överordnade chef är verksamhetsområdeschefen.
   

  

 Tjänsten innebär att du hanterar sekretessbelagda uppgifter vilket ställer höga krav på rätt hantering. Därför är det viktigt att du som ska arbeta hos oss har högt säkerhetsmedvetande.
   

  

 Det är ett jobb för dig som vill vara med och ta ansvar.
   

  

 Vi söker dig som
   

  

* har högskoleexamen eller erfarenheter och kvalifikationer som kan prövas likvärdiga
* har tidigare chefserfarenhet
* har god ledarskapsförmåga
* utvecklar långsiktiga och positiva målbilder
* tar egna initiativ och agerar proaktivt, får saker att hända och tar ansvar för resultatet
* förstår organisationens struktur och kultur och tar tillvara kompetenser effektivt
* kan förstå och analysera komplexa frågor
* samarbetar effektivt med andra för att nå gemensamma mål
* har god självkännedom och är stabil som person, främjar ett förtroendeskapande arbetsklimat
* främjar ömsesidig dialog, är lyhörd och anpassar sin kommunikation efter behov och situation.


 Det är meriterande om du
   

  

* har erfarenhet av operativt och strategiskt ledarskap
* har erfarenhet av ledarskap i liknande verksamhet
* har arbetat med verksamhetsutveckling
* har erfarenhet av att leda utvecklingsinitiativ med IT-inslag
* har erfarenhet av att leda på distans
* har erfarenhet av nära samarbete med kollegor på distans.


 Vi lägger stor vikt vid dina personliga egenskaper.
   

  

 Övrigt
   

  

 En tjänst, tillsvidare, heltid, med tillträde enligt överenskommelse. Provanställning kan bli aktuellt. I enlighet med Försäkringskassans riktlinje för säkerhet genomförs bakgrundskontroller i denna rekrytering. Möjlighet till distansarbete finns om arbetet tillåter. I den här rekryteringen kan arbetspsykologiska test användas som en del i urvalsarbetet. Placeringsort: Stockholm.
   

  

 Vill du veta mer om Försäkringskassan som arbetsgivare? Besök fk.se/jobbahososs
   

  

 Kontaktpersoner
   

  

 Chef: Daniel Hansson, 010-116 40 09 (för frågor om tjänsten). HR-specialist: Malin Skoglund, malin.skoglund2@forsakringskassan.se, 010-113 10 45 (för frågor om rekryteringsprocessen). ST: Behzad Barkhodaee, 010-116 22 96, Saco-S: Peter Moberg, 010-111 79 29, Seko: Eliza Grundström, 010-115 34 02.
   

  

 Ansökan
   

  

 Välkommen med din ansökan senast den 26 maj 2026. Ladda gärna upp dina examensbevis, betyg och intyg i samband med din ansökan.
   

  

 Har du skyddade personuppgifter eller ingen tillgång till bank-id ringer du den HR-specialist som finns namngiven i annonsen så får du hjälp med att skicka in din ansökan.
   

  

 Vi ska i allt vi gör, agera med utgångspunkt i principen om alla människors lika värde, rättssäkerhet och med god service.

title: HP Growth Manager (temporary)
description:
Take charge of growing the HP business in Sweden’s commercial and public segment, driving strategic initiatives, building strong relationships with HP and Dustin account managers, and making a tangible impact on business development success.
 **Note: This is a temporary contract for 12-months, with the possibility of extension.**
**This is what makes the position as HP Growth Manager unique**
* Drive growth of HP's Client Portfolio business by building strong, strategic relationships
* Identify new business opportunities using HP data, tools, and leads to actively support commercial sales growth
* Take ownership of driving HP business growth and creating impactful initiatives that deliver measurable results


 In this role, you will be responsible for driving the growth of the Client Portfolio business and strengthening relationships with our strategic vendor, HP.
