title: Account Executive
description:
**About TicketCo** 

 At TicketCo, our vision is to create the best fan journey in the world - from ticket purchase to matchday experience.
 



 We empower ticketing teams with powerful, intuitive technology that simplifies operations, increases revenue, and elevates the customer experience. The ticketing teams behind the scenes are heroes, and they deserve best-in-class tools.
 



 With a leading position in the Nordics and rapid growth in the UK sports market, TicketCo is entering an exciting new chapter. Sweden is a key strategic growth market for us - and Sales plays a critical role in driving new business, expanding our footprint, and winning the right clubs.
 



 We are now looking for a driven Account Executive in Sweden who wants to hunt new business, close meaningful deals, and grow their career in a fast-scaling SaaS company.
 



  




**The Opportunity** 

 You will identify, pursue, and close deals with Swedish sports organisations and event organisers. You will own your pipeline end-to-end: from outbound prospecting to demos to signed contracts. You are not afraid to pick up the phone, walk into a stadium, or pitch at the boardroom table.
 



 You will work closely with Customer Success, Product, and Marketing and carry real revenue targets from day one. For the right person, this role offers rapid career growth into senior sales, team lead, or commercial leadership.
 



  




**What You Will Do** 

 Drive New Business
 


* Own your full sales cycle - from first outreach to signed contract.
* Proactively identify and qualify new club opportunities across Sweden.
* Build and manage a robust pipeline, consistently hitting activity and revenue targets.
* Conduct compelling product demos tailored to each club's commercial goals.
* Negotiate and close deals with decision-makers at all levels - from ticketing managers to CEOs.



 Commercial Strategy & Positioning
 


* Deeply understand each prospect's business model, revenue challenges, and fan engagement goals.
* Position TicketCo as the platform of choice against competing solutions.
* Collaborate with Customer Success to ensure smooth handovers and fast time-to-value.
* Provide market feedback to Product and Marketing based on frontline sales insights.



 Relationship Building
 


* Build a strong personal network across Swedish sports - clubs and leagues.
* Represent TicketCo at industry events, matches, and networking opportunities.
* Act as a brand ambassador for TicketCo in the Swedish market.


**Who You Are** 
* A natural hunter - you are energised by prospecting, pitching, and closing.
* 3–5 years of experience in B2B SaaS sales OR as a Commercial Manager / Revenue-focused role at a professional sports club.
* Genuinely passionate about sports - you understand the world of clubs, fans, and matchday operations from the inside.
* Track record of hitting or exceeding revenue targets in a new business or commercial role.
* Strong communicator and storyteller - you can make complex technology feel simple and relevant.
* Comfortable navigating long sales cycles and multiple stakeholders, from operations to ownership.
* Self-driven, resilient, and comfortable with the uncertainty that comes with hunting new business.
* Fluent in Swedish, with solid working English.
* Experience from SaaS, sports tech, events, media rights, sponsorship, or ticketing is a strong advantage - but if you are coming from a club background with strong commercial instincts, we want to hear from you.



 Experience from SaaS, sports, events, ticketing, or recurring/transaction-based business models is a strong advantage, but not a requirement.
 



  




**Why Join TicketCo Sweden?** 
* Work with the most exciting event organisers in Sweden - and help them grow.
* Carry real revenue responsibility and make a direct commercial impact.
* Competitive salary.
* Strong career progression in a scaling international SaaS company.
* High ownership, autonomy, and trust - we back our people.
* Collaborative and ambitious team culture with colleagues across Nordics and UKI.
* Hybrid flexible setup in Stockholm.
* Travel to clubs, stadiums, and industry events across Sweden and beyond.

title: Qlik Engineer
description:
We are seeking a skilled and proactive CDC Qlik Replicate Administrator with 10 to 14 years of experience to oversee the administration and operational management of our Change Data Capture (CDC) platform using Qlik Replicate. The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring the platform's stability, performance, and scalability, while supporting data replication processes across various source and target systems.
 



**Key Responsibilities:** 




**Platform Administration:** 




 Install, configure, and maintain Qlik Replicate servers and agents.
 



 Manage user access, roles, and permissions within the Qlik Replicate environment.
 



 Monitor system health, performance, and resource utilization.
 



 Apply patches, upgrades, and security updates as needed.
 



**Operational Management:** 




 Manage and monitor CDC tasks and replication jobs across multiple environments.
 



 Troubleshoot and resolve replication failures, latency issues, and data inconsistencies.
 



 Ensure high availability and disaster recovery configurations are in place.
 



 Maintain logs, audit trails, and documentation for operational activities.
 



**Support & Collaboration:** 




 Work closely with data engineering, application teams, and DBAs to support integration requirements.
 



 Provide technical support and guidance to stakeholders on Qlik Replicate capabilities and best practices.
 



 Participate in incident management and root cause analysis for replication-related issues.
 



**Automation & Optimization:** 




 Develop scripts and automation tools for routine tasks and monitoring.
 



 Optimize replication tasks for performance and resource efficiency.
 



 Implement alerting and proactive monitoring solutions.
 



**Compliance & Governance:** 




 Ensure data replication processes comply with internal policies and external regulations.
 



 Maintain documentation for audits and compliance reviews.
 



**Required Qualifications:** 




 Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, or related field.
 



 7+ years of experience in administering Qlik Replicate or similar CDC platforms.
 



 Strong understanding of data replication concepts, ETL/ELT processes, and database technologies (e.g., Oracle, SQL Server, PostgreSQL, SAP, etc.).
 



 Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and hybrid environments.
 



 Proficiency in scripting (PowerShell, Python, Bash) for automation and monitoring.
 



 Familiarity with networking, firewalls, and security protocols related to data movement.
 



**Preferred Skills:** 




 Qlik Replicate certification or equivalent.
 



 Experience with enterprise monitoring tools (e.g., Splunk, Prometheus, Grafana).
 



 Knowledge of data governance and compliance frameworks (GDPR, HIPAA, etc.).
 



 Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
 



 Excellent communication and documentation abilities.

title: PD Project Manager - Vehicle Service Information
description:
TRATON is a group of strong brands with a shared mission: transforming transportation together to create the future of sustainable transport solutions. Within TRATON, we include MAN, Scania, Volkswagen Truck & Bus, and International. As part of a global team of industry experts, you get to think bigger, experience more, and reach further. Together, we have the power to transform transportation - Let´s make a difference together. Find out more: www.traton.com
   

  

  

  

 Our values – customer first, respect, team spirit, responsibility, and elimination of waste – are at the heart of everything we do.
   

  

**Role Summary**
 We offer a position in an organization that provides both insight and involvement in the customer value we deliver. Right now, we are in an exciting phase as we intensify collaboration within TRATON. Our work involves coordinating with the main development flow in R&D to ensure the right quality from a service perspective.
   

  

 As a project manager, you support the teams in delivering results. Your contribution includes coordination, structure, process management and problem-solving. Communication is essential, as you are expected to report status and represent the project both internally and to other departments.
   

  

**Job Responsibilities**
* Manage and lead the team in organizing, monitoring and executing current and future projects related to vehicle service information
* Work closely with various cross-functional teams to ensure seamless integration and functionality of service information
* Overall delivery planning towards deadlines
* Analyze and present consequences of deviations from plan
* Act on and escalate risks and deviations for the project
* Actively participate in planning and other specialist tasks to support the team’s goals


**Who You Are**
* You have an engineering degree or equivalent experience in a similar role.
* You have previous experience as a project leader preferably from product development projects. Experience from Scania project management is extra meritorious.
* You are fluent in English and Swedish.
* Comfortable working in a dynamic environment where both agile and traditional development methods are used.
* Experience and knowledge in agile methods.
* You can see the big picture and help the organization prioritize and improve work methods and processes when needed.


 We are looking for a natural leader who motivates others and can easily collaborate with people in various positions within the company. You can see the big picture, be holistic and look both upstream and downstream the flow as well as help the organization prioritize and improve work methods and processes when needed.
   

  

**This Is Us**
 Our department plays a central role by providing the information, tools and systems required to maintain, diagnose, repair, and upgrade transport solutions in a safe, high-quality and cost-effective manner.
   

  

 We also have an important role in product development by offering expert knowledge and support to design in order to ensure appropriate solutions from a service perspective and, ultimately, to give Traton workshop network the conditions needed to maximize the availability of our transport solutions.
   

  

 We are a team working across several exciting areas. Our backgrounds are varied and we have a wide range of experience and expertise. We develop together and share experiences and knowledge to support each other in daily work.
   

  

**TRATON Offers**
 We offer a dynamic, flexible workplace with hybrid work options, including Scania Sergel and Midway hubs. With a structured development plan and courses, TRATON Group R&D supports your career growth both locally and internationally.
   

  

 Benefits include wellness allowance, bonus, flexible hours, and company car leasing. We also host events for employees and their families, and Stockholm residents enjoy direct access to Södertälje via Scania Job express buses for an easy commute to Södertälje.
   

  

**Application**
 Your application should include a CV and any relevant certificates. Important: Upload your CV in English. Please note that we are not able to process any applications sent by email, due to GDPR regulations. We welcome all applicants and strive for diversity in our recruitment processes.
   

  

 If you have questions or would like more information about the position, please contact: Nenad Momic, Manager TGROSS, nenad.momic@scania.com.
   

  

 Apply as soon as possible, no later than 2026-05-26. Screening will take place on an ongoing basis during the application period. Logical and personality tests may be used as part of the selection process, and a background check may be required for this role.
   

  

 We look forward to your application!
   

  

 This recruitment process is handled by Scania for TRATON R&D

title: Backend Engineer
description:
**We are looking for a Backend Engineer Who Wants To Make Finance Fun and Simple**
 At Lendo, we're driven by a mission that is crystal clear and truly exciting: to make the world of finance simple and fair for our customers! Our work is all about shining a light on complex financial decisions and ensuring the user always comes out on top. We put
 **Customer Centric** 
 at the heart of every decision—no confusing internal politics, just pure value, transparency, and simplicity. We have a clear strategy and
 **Focus on Execution** 
 ; we put the "DO" in Lendo and get things done, prioritizing speed and good results over perfect and slow!
   

  

**Our Culture – Our Superpowers!**
 How do we pull this off? By living our core values. We know we can only be the #1 if we lift each other up and
 **Win as a Team** 
 . Here, we share knowledge, support each other, set high (but fun!) expectations, learn from our mistakes, and celebrate when we achieve our goals. Yes, we have fun while we win!
   

  

 We also maintain an incredibly curious mindset and strive to
 **Always Improve** 
 . We work smart, we try hard, we celebrate testing new ideas, and insights are our guide to success. We are convinced that diverse perspectives, differences, and a strong collective drive are what allow us to continue evolving. We are always looking for colleagues who want to grow together with us!
   

  

**About The Role**
 You'll be a key player in our cross-functional, open-minded, and agile product team. Imagine being the brain behind the scalable backend services that power the user journeys central to the Lendo platform.
   

  

**As a Backend Engineer, you will:**
* Design, build, and optimize our Go microservices to be super scalable.
* Take end-to-end ownership of the backend architecture.
* Write clear, testable, and maintainable code that ensures quality and reliability
* Collaborate closely with Product Managers, Designers, and other engineers to deliver customer value
* Identify and track key metrics to constantly improve system performance.
* Continuously experiment, learn, and share knowledge with your team.
* Bring your ideas to life together with the team


**About You**
 We believe you have:
   

  

* 5+ years of hands-on backend development experience, including building and maintaining production systems.
* Strong skills in at least one backend language; ideally Go, but we know great engineers can learn quickly!
* A deep care for code quality, rigorous testing, and system scalability.
* Solid hands-on experience working with tools like Docker, Kubernetes, Git, and GitHub Actions (or similar CI/CD systems).
* A natural team player mindset where code reviews and clean code are central to your quality process.


**Tech Talk:** 
 Our scalable backend uses Go microservices and AMQP for asynchronous messaging. We are cloud-native, leveraging Docker and Kubernetes on GCP, with continuous support from our CI/CD pipelines.
   

  

**Good to know about the recruitment:** 
 As part of our recruitment process we perform mandatory background check on all final candidates (we will always inform you as a candidate before the check is carried out).
   

  

 Are you ready to
 **Win as a Team** 
 and put
 **Customer Centric** 
 in every line of code? We look forward to your application!

title: SAP SD/MM Solution Architect
description:
If you enjoy working with high-caliber people on interesting projects in a thriving partnership organization, Westernacher is the place to be!
   

  

 To complement our team in
 **Sweden** 
 , we are looking for a
 **SAP SD/MM Solution Architect.**
**Your Responsibilities**
* Delivering S/4HANA transformations and lead a SAP consultant project team
* SAP SD/ MM process design solutions incl. customizing, documentation and testing
* Integrating SD/MM with other SAP Modules
* Leading customer workshops
* Preparing blueprint and functional specifications
* Managing test conduction process and go live
* Participating in pre-sales activities
* Leading, training and mentor a team


**Your Skills And Experience**
* 7+ years of implementing the SAP SD/MM module
* Functional/ technical proficiency with at least three or more full-cycle SAP implementations
* Certification in the SAP SD/ MM modules is an asset
* Excellent skills at implementing technology-enabled business solutions for clients
* Exceptional communication and customer-management skills
* Team mentorship or leadership skills
* Project management experience and pre-sales experience are an asset
* Excellent communication and presentation skills in Swedish and English - other languages are an advantage
* Available to travel if the project requires it


**Westernacher's Promise**
* A diverse and versatile role with ample opportunities to shape your work in a 100% employee-owned company
* Competitive remuneration package
* Inspiring and exciting, innovative working environment
* Partnership culture with a strong entrepreneurial spirit and opportunity for partnership/co-ownership
* Environment of open communication, trust and regular feedback
* International company culture and minimal hierarchical structure
* Chance to work on complex projects from different industries and add to your business process knowhow
* Flexible working hours and hybrid working model


**Interested? Then, don't hesitate to apply!**
**This Is WE**
 Committed to innovation, since 1969 Westernacher Consulting operates worldwide, with more than 1000 consultants in Europe, Asia and the Americas. We provide global business and SAP consulting, serving medium to large sized enterprises striving for innovation, operational excellence and profitable, sustainable growth. Headquartered in Heidelberg, Germany, Westernacher Consulting is the global leader in business process and technology innovation.
   

  

 Living, working, and operating in partnership. We are inquisitive, creative thinkers with a strong entrepreneurial spirit. We question new ways of working, think differently and are willing to try out new approaches to create value and innovation for our customers. All of this makes us pioneers in our field and keeps us responsive, quality-focused and results-oriented.
   

  

 Westernacher is a diverse work environment and is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. Westernacher is also committed to compliance with all fair employment practices regarding citizenship and immigration status.
   

  

 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Automation Engineer
description:
Our Equation for Success
  
Over the last 31 years Gyptech (Gyptech AB) has become a global leader in the design, construction, commissioning and servicing of wallboard equipment and plaster plants.
  
  

**We believe:** Innovation + Perseverance + Caring + Humility = Excellence
  
Our team of highly experienced management, engineering, operations and manufacturing professionals guarantees success through innovative design, manufacturing and a commitment to exceeding customer expectations. We have big plans for growth and are on the lookout for the best talent to help make them happen.
  
What We Offer
  
The value of rewards our employees receive goes well beyond a regular pay cheque. We provide:
  
* Competitive compensation
* Company supported continuing education and opportunities for growth
* Participation in developing industry-leading innovation with the freedom to be creative and take initiative
* A phenomenal team driven to be the best (while still having fun!)
* A state of the art, modern work environment
* A collaborative organizational structure which encourages input and feedback from all employees
* Casual work atmosphere and a flexible work schedule
* Opportunity to travel and work on exciting and challenging large-scale, global projects


What You’ll Do
  
Gyptech is seeking an Control Systems Engineer (Automationsingenjör) to join our Electrical Engineering & Controls team. In this role, you will develop and commission automation systems for industrial manufacturing equipment, ensuring high performance, reliability, and operational excellence.
  
  

**Your role will involve:**  

* Design configure and program PLC-based control systems (Ladder, Structured Text, Function Blocks) for industrial equipment
* Develop HMI/SCADA solutions for automated production environment
* Support electrical drive systems, PLC panel design, and equipment documentation (e.g. equipment motor and device list)
* Understanding safety risk assessments and design
* Design and support industrial network and data collection systems


Participate in international commissioning, installation and trouble shooting
  
This position is based out of our office in Växjö, Sweden with a requirement to travel internationally on a consistent basis.
  
  

What You Offer
  
* Minimum 5 years of hands-on controls development and commissioning experience in a manufacturing environment
* Demonstrated familiarity with Rockwell Software (ControlLogix, RSLinx, FTView), Siemens (Step7 v5.5, TIA Portal, WinCC), Invensys Wonderware/Archestra, GE iFix, Ignition
* Familiarity with ethernet network concepts and troubleshooting
* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
* Strong interpersonal and communication skills
* Excellent organization skills and prioritization to meet deadlines
* Ability to work independently and collaboratively
* Industrial robots, process controls, instrumentation or Functional Safety experience is an asset


How to Apply
  
Qualified applicants please submit a resume to hr@company.com and quote the position title in the subject line.
  
  

A Bit More About Us
  
With offices in North America, Europe and South America, we have been responsible for supplying the equipment for the majority of new production volumes over the last decade. Our experience designing and commissioning equipment globally, means that we are prepared to meet the unique requirements presented by each of our customers. Our wallboard plants serve a variety of production capacities and surpass expectations of safety, quality and performance in any market.
  
  

For questions regarding this opportunity, please reach out to Vivian Nguyen (HR Coordinator) at hr@gyptech.ca. To learn more about our organization, please visit www.gyptech.com

title: Founding Engineer, n8n Labs
description:
**The AI orchestration of your wildest imagination.**


n8n is the open workflow orchestration platform built for the new era of AI. We give technical teams the freedom of code with the speed of no-code, so they can automate faster, smarter, and without limits. Backed by a fiercely inventive community and 500+ builder-approved integrations, we’re changing the way people bring systems together and scale ideas for impact.


Since our founding in 2019, we’ve grown into a diverse team of over 220 - working across Europe and the US, connected by a shared builder spirit and with our centre of gravity in Berlin. Along the way, we’ve:

* Cultivated a community of more than 650,000 active developers and builders
* Earned 145k+ GitHub stars, making us one of the world’s Top 40 most popular projects
* Been ranked as one of Europe’s most promising privately held SaaS startups (4th in Sifted’s 2025 B2B SaaS Rising 100)
* Raised $240m to date, from Sequoia’s first German seed to our recent $180m Series C - bringing us to a $2.5bn valuation

  

That’s the company we’ve built. Now we’d love to see what *you* can build. If you’re applying, try n8n out - whether you’re technical or not - and share a screenshot of your first workflow with us. The easiest place to start is here: app.n8n.cloud/register.


We’re in a defining moment of an incredible journey. Come and build with us.


Your main goal will be to meaningfully increase the probability that n8n wins in the AI-native future by rapidly identifying, validating, and building the next generation of AI-native products and experiences.


To do so, you’ll operate as part of a small, high-caliber skunkworks team focused on creating entirely new products from zero:

### **Explore the future of AI-powered work**

* Continuously explore emerging shifts in AI interfaces, orchestration, agents, and autonomy through hands-on experimentation and ecosystem research.
* Stay deeply connected to the AI ecosystem through founder networks, user conversations, Product Hunt, Reddit, GitHub, X, events, and emerging tooling.
* Identify unmet needs and future opportunities that could shape n8n’s long-term product direction.

### **Build and launch 0 1 products**

* Rapidly prototype, validate, and launch new AI-native product ideas with minimal support and high autonomy.
* Operate with a highly scrappy, execution-first mindset across product, design, engineering, and go-to-market.
* Pull in the right tooling, people, contractors, or external support when needed to move quickly and unblock progress.

### **Evaluate opportunities and shape strategic bets**

* Use structured thinking, research, and experimentation to evaluate what n8n should invest in over the next 1–3 years.
* Validate ideas through user conversations, market signals, prototypes, and fast feedback loops rather than long planning cycles.
* Bring strong product intuition and reasoning to help leadership navigate ambiguity and emerging AI trends.

### **Shape strategic product bets for n8n**

* Work closely with n8n’s founder and Product leadership to workshop ideas, evaluate opportunities, and pressure-test assumptions.
* Bring structured thinking, research, and experimentation to help guide future product investment decisions.
* Share insights and discoveries with the wider organization to help n8n stay ahead of the AI landscape.

**REQUIREMENTS**
----------------

### **Must-haves**

* **0 1 product building experience:** You’ve built and launched products from scratch, ideally in the AI space, and know how to move from idea to real-world validation quickly.
* **AI-native product intuition:** You deeply understand how AI is reshaping user behavior, workflows, interfaces, and product experiences.
* **Hands-on execution:** You’re comfortable building, prototyping, experimenting, and operating with minimal structure or support.
* **Product intuition & problem-solving:** You combine strong product instincts with structured thinking and can quickly validate whether an idea is worth pursuing.
* **Product vision:** You’re known for spotting non-obvious opportunities and thinking ahead of where the market is going.
* **Speed & resourcefulness:** You thrive in ambiguity, move fast, and know how to creatively unblock yourself to make progress.
* **Collaboration & communication:** You can workshop ideas with senior leaders, challenge assumptions thoughtfully, and bring others along through clear reasoning.

### **Nice-to-haves**

* **Deep AI ecosystem immersion:** You actively follow emerging products, models, startups, communities, and technical trends across the AI landscape.
* **Ecosystem curiosity:** You naturally stay plugged into what’s happening across podcasts, X, Reddit, Product Hunt, events, and founder/operator communities.
* **Founder experience:** You’ve previously founded or been an early employee at a startup and understand what it takes to build under uncertainty.
* **Technical breadth:** You’re comfortable using AI coding tools, low-code/no-code tooling, or building lightweight prototypes independently.

**Why join us?**


At n8n, you won’t be optimizing a roadmap or shipping incremental features. You’ll help define what the next generation of AI-native software looks like inside one of Europe’s fastest-growing technology companies - operating more like a founder than a traditional product leader.


You’ll join a small, highly autonomous team with direct exposure to company leadership and the freedom to explore bold ideas, challenge assumptions, and build products from scratch. Your work will influence the long-term direction of n8n and could shape entirely new product lines for the company.


You’ll operate in a remote-first culture that values speed, experimentation, ownership, and builder energy - with the backing, distribution, and ambition of a company serving hundreds of thousands of developers and technical teams worldwide.


Sound like the kind of challenge you’ve been looking for?


Apply now - and help us build the future of AI orchestration.

*n8n is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.*

*We can sponsor visas to Germany; for any other country, you need to have existing right to work.*

***Our company language is English.***

*You care about diversity and inclusion? We do too! Check out our* *Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging initiatives at n8n* *(**https://www.notion.so/n8n/Diversity-inclusion-and-belonging-n8n-c1bec2fff536422d868b1a438d990e35**).*

***Location disclaimer:*** *If you see multiple job postings for the same role, it is most likely because we're hiring remotely for this role and posting in different locations to make sure every potential candidate can see the role. Please apply to the location you're the most likely to work from in the future.*

**Benefits**
------------

* **Competitive compensation** – We offer fair and attractive pay.
* **Ownership** – Our core value is to “empower others,” and we mean it—you’ll get a slice of n8n with equity.
* **Work/life balance** – We work hard but ensure you have time to recharge:


	+ **Europe:** 30 days of vacation, plus public holidays wherever you are.
	+ **US:** 20 vacation days, 8 sick days, plus public holidays wherever you are.
* **Health & wellness** –


	+ **Europe:** We provide benefits according to local country norms.\*
	+ **US**: Multiple low-premium, low-deductible medical plans with coverage for individuals and families—plus a no-cost premium HDHP option with a pre-seeded HSA—along with dental and vision coverage.
* **Future planning** –


	+ **Europe:** We provide pension contributions according to local country norms.\*
	+ **US:** 401(k) retirement plan with a 4% employer match.
* **Financial security** –


	+ **Europe:** We provide benefits according to local country norms.\*
	+ **US:** Company-paid short-term and long-term disability insurance, plus life insurance to support you and your loved ones.
* **Career growth** – We hire rising stars who grow with us! You’ll get €1K (or equivalent) per year to spend on courses, books, events, or coaching to level up your skills.
* **A passionate team** – We love our product, and we prove it with regular hackathons where we see who can build the coolest thing with it!
* **Remote-first** – Our team works remotely across Europe, with regular off-sites for team bonding. Some roles, like sales in the US, are hybrid—please check the job description.
* **Giving back** – We're big fans of open source, and you'll get $100 per month to support projects you care about.
* **AI enablement –** We believe in working smarter—everyone gets an unlimited AI budget to explore and use the best tools to boost productivity and creativity.
* **Transparency** – We all know what everyone’s working on, how the company is doing—the whole shebang.
* **An ambitious but kind culture** – People love working here—our eNPS for 2024 is 94!
* *Country-specific details are provided in your contract.*

title: SIL-ingenjör inom Testautomation och Verifiering
description:
Vill du arbeta med framtidens avancerade förarstödssystem och bidra till säkrare fordon? Vi söker nu en SIL Engineer som vill vara med och utveckla och förbättra test- och verifieringsflöden för ADAS-funktioner i en högteknologisk och agil miljö.
  
I rollen får du arbeta med både praktisk testning och strategiskt förbättringsarbete inom testautomation, kvalitetssäkring och CI/CD.
  
Om rollenSom SIL Engineer blir du en viktig del av teamet som ansvarar för validering och verifiering av avancerade förarstödssystem såsom collision avoidance, driver alert och connected safety.
  
Du kommer arbeta med att utveckla testmetoder, automatisera verifieringsflöden och säkerställa hög kvalitet i komplexa embedded-system. Rollen innefattar arbete i både SIL- och HIL-miljöer med stort fokus på automation och effektivisering.
  
Du samarbetar nära utvecklingsteam, testledare och systemansvariga i en modern och snabbföränderlig utvecklingsmiljö.Dina arbetsuppgifter kommer bland annat innefatta:
  
Skapa och vidareutveckla testmetoder baserat på systemkrav
  
Utforma testscenarier och definiera förväntade testresultat
  
Utföra felsökning och felanalys i SIL- och HIL-miljöer
  
Automatisera testfall och integrera dem i CI/CD-pipelines
  
Ansvara för och vidareutveckla regressionstestsviter
  
Utföra manuell testning i olika testmiljöer vid behov
  
Mäta, analysera och följa upp test coverage och kvalitet
  
Bidra med feedback kring krav och förbättringsmöjligheter
  
  

För att lyckas i rollen ser vi gärna att du jobbat med flera av tidigare verktyg:
  
Python och dataanalys (exempelvis Pandas)
  
Testautomationsramverk och CI/CD-flöden
  
SIL/HIL-testmiljöer
  
Open Scenario / Open Drive
  
Verktyg för analys och felsökning av embedded systems
  
Vi söker dig somHar minst 2 års erfarenhet av test, verifiering eller utveckling inom embedded systems
  
Har arbetat med SIL (Software-in-the-loop), HIL är meriterande
  
Är van vid testautomation och scripting i Python
  
Har god analytisk förmåga och erfarenhet av felsökning
  
Trivs i agila team med högt tempo och nära samarbete
  
Meriterande erfarenheterErfarenhet av ADAS/AD-funktioner
  
Erfarenhet av CI/CD pipelines och regressionstestning
  
Förmåga att snabbt sätta sig in i nya miljöer och bidra från start
  
  

**OM ANSTÄLLNINGEN:**  

Detta är ett konsultuppdrag vilket innebär att du kommer vara anställd hos oss på Friday med målet att på sikt bli överrekryterad av kunden.
  
**ÖVRIG INFO:** Omfattning: Heltid
  
**Start:** Omgående/Enligt överrenskommelse
  
**Lön:** Fast månadslön
  
**Placering:** Göteborg
  
**Rekryteringsansvarig:** Stina Högström
  
  

Ansök genom att klicka på "Sök tjänst". Kom ihåg att vara snabb med din ansökan då vi gör löpande urval av kandidater och att annonsen kan stängs ner innan tjänsten är tillsatt om vi gått över till urvals och intervjufasen.
  
  

**OM FRIDAY:**  

På Friday hjälper vi Tech-talanger att hitta roller där de trivs, utvecklas och gör skillnad. Genom att lyssna på vad just du vill och drivs av, matchar vi dig med företag och möjligheter som får dig att se fram emot att gå till jobbet.
  
Vi värderar ambition och potential högt och arbetar aktivt för en fördomsfri rekrytering, där alla ges samma chans att lyckas. Vi finns i Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Linköping och Örebro.
  
Varje år genomför vi över 5 000 karriärmöten och matchar kandidater med allt från globala företag till innovativa startups.

title: Robotics Perception Engineer
description:
As a Robotics Perception Engineer, you will develop and integrate advanced perception capabilities that enable subsea situational awareness and autonomous operations.
  
You will contribute across the perception stack, including multi-modal sensing, 3D scene understanding, tracking, anomaly detection and real-time inference. The work spans simulation, R&D prototyping, embedded deployment and real-world testing on ROVs, AUVs and vessel-based systems.
  
Key tasks and responsibilities
  
Research, design and implement perception algorithms tailored for subsea robotics
  
Develop modules for 3D object detection, segmentation, tracking, anomaly detection and environment understanding
  
Address challenges related to degraded sensor data and harsh marine conditions
  
Integrate perception capabilities into autonomy, navigation and control frameworks to support mission-level decision making
  
Participate in simulation, testing, validation and full-scale field trials
  
Drive perception-driven innovation and transition research into production-ready operational capabilities
  
Contribute to documentation, standards and continuous improvement processes
  
  

What you bring
  
Degree in Robotics, Computer Vision, Mathematics or a related engineering field
  
Strong experience with multi-modal perception (vision, lidar/sonar, radar, acoustic sensing)
  
Proficiency in C++ and Python for robotics development
  
Familiarity with ROS 2, real-time systems, sensor calibration and perception lifecycle development
  
Experience with machine-learning-based perception methods is valuable
  
  

Experience
  
3–5 years of experience within perception, sensor fusion or autonomous system development
  
Hands-on experience designing and deploying perception algorithms on embedded or constrained compute hardware
  
Experience with 3D object detection, segmentation, tracking and calibration in real-world operational conditions
  
Understanding of integrating perception with planning, controls and autonomy pipelines
  
Experience with CI/CD workflows, GPU-accelerated processing, simulation tools and field validation is an advantage
  
Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across mechanical, electrical, software and operations teams
  
  

Why join Reach Subsea?
  
Your perception algorithms will be used in real subsea missions — not just simulated environments
  
A truly multidisciplinary engineering environment spanning sensing, autonomy, mechanics and offshore operations
  
High degree of technical influence on perception architecture and capability direction
  
A growing organization with strong ambitions within autonomy, subsea digitalization and sustainable operations
  
Flexible work locations (Gothenburg, Oslo, Haugesund or Bergen)
  
Competitive compensation and strong opportunities for professional growth

title: Controls & Automation Engineer (m/f/d)
description:
Mission Brief
  
You will maintain, troubleshoot and upgrade the control system of the Isar rocket engine test facility near Kiruna (Sweden). You will assume technical responsibility for both software and hardware elements of the control system. There will be close collaboration with the Isar Munich control systems team for work on both this facility, and other future projects.
  
You will be involved in day-to-day test operations, and will work alongside Propulsion Test Engineers to operate the facility. It is expected that there will be a close connection to the other test engineers, and development of a deep understanding of facility systems and requirements.
  
  

**Your Role in Our Space Mission:**  

Develop PLC software for existing and future control systems in Kiruna
  
Design PLC hardware setups and PLC-related networks
  
Perform risk analyses and design safety-critical electrical systems
  
Commission control systems and maintain/upgrade them
  
Be responsible for hardware and software related topics on site
  
Perform rocket engine tests as part of the local test engineering and operations team
  
  

Qualification Checklist
  
Either a degree in electrical engineering, automation technology, or similar, or a certified engineer in automation technology
  
2+ years of experience in automation and PLC technology
  
Fluent in English
  
Experience in the field of PLC programming (soft-PLC) and commissioning preferably using Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 or CODESYS based systems
  
Experience in giving technical support and troubleshooting in the field and remotely
  
Knowledge about fieldbus systems
  
Basic understanding of electrical schematics
  
  

Bonus Skills
  
Knowledge of sensors/sensor signals and test equipment
  
Experience designing and building PLC systems to EU and international standards, preferably with Beckhoff controllers
  
Experience with functional safety system design and implementation

title: Engineer: Should Costing
description:
**Job Requirements**  

At Quest Global, it’s not just what we do but how and why we do it that makes us different. Our people are driven by the desire to make the world a better place—to make a positive difference that contributes to a brighter future. **Key Responsibilities**

We are known for our extraordinary people who make the impossible possible every day. Questians are driven by hunger, humility, and aspiration. * Ability to quickly learn and deliver the tasks with prescribed quality levels.

title: Doctoral student in Intentional Monitoring of Agentic AI Software
description:
Join our division and AI Engineering Lab to be at the forefront of research in agentic AI software systems. This PhD project aims to observe and monitor agentic AI systems using intentional visual models – models where intentions or goals are explicit and evaluated during system operations. The PhD student will be supervised by Assoc. Prof. Jennifer Horkoff and will be part of the Interaction Design and Software Engineering Division of the Department of Computer Science and Engineering.


**About us**
------------



The Department of Computer Science and Engineering, a joint department of Chalmers and the University of Gothenburg, spans the breadth of computing disciplines. Our internationally visible research, strong industry links and diverse environment create a collaborative setting where ideas grow into real impact.



At the division of Interaction Design and Software Engineering, we design smarter ways to engineer better software, and explore how people engage with digital systems, combining global research perspectives with strong collaboration with industry.



The PhD position will be part of the Chalmers and University of Gothenburg’s AI Engineering Lab, focusing on practices, knowledge, and experience making AI advancements work in real and complex software systems. The PhD student will part of the WASP graduate school.



Wallenberg AI, Autonomous Systems and Software Program (WASP) is Sweden’s largest individual research program ever, a major national initiative for strategically motivated basic research, education and faculty recruitment. The program addresses research on artificial intelligence and autonomous systems acting in collaboration with humans, adapting to their environment through sensors, information and knowledge, and forming intelligent systems-of-systems.



The vision of WASP is excellent research and competence in artificial intelligence, autonomous systems and software for the benefit of Swedish society and industry.



Read more: https://wasp-sweden.org/


**WASP graduate school**
------------------------



The graduate school within WASP is dedicated to provide the skills needed to analyze, develop, and contribute to the interdisciplinary area of artificial intelligence, autonomous systems and software. Through an ambitious program with research visits, partner universities, and visiting lecturers, the graduate school actively supports forming a strong multi-disciplinary and international professional network between PhD-students, researchers and industry.



Read more: https://wasp-sweden.org/graduate-school/


**About the research project**
------------------------------



Agentic AI systems allow multiple foundation-model powered agents to work together, often using external tools, to accomplish system objectives. AI agent configurations can be complicated and dynamic, with many possible configurations of agents, agent types, tasks, inputs and outputs, including human oversight, memory, and mechanisms for reflection and improvement. Thus, there is a need to observe the status of the system in terms of current agent roles and tasks, goals (intentions), inputs and outputs, in order to evaluate whether the goals of individual agents and the overall agentic system are satisfied, including whether improvement mechanisms are effective. The overall objective of this PhD proposal is to support automated and intentional visualization, observability, and monitoring of agentic AI systems. We propose to use and adapt existing and well-established work in agent- and goal-oriented modeling frameworks for this purpose.


**Who we are looking for**
--------------------------


#### ***The following requirements are mandatory:***


* To qualify as a Doctoral student, you must have a Master's degree (*masterexamen*) of 120 credits or a Master’s degree (*magisterexamen*) of 60 credits\* in **Computer Science, Software Engineering, or AI Engineering**.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.
* Experience in software engineering, applied AI, and conceptual modeling.
* The ability to work both independently and collaboratively within a research group.


* *for students with an education earned outside of Sweden, a 4-year Bachelor’s degree is accepted.*


#### ***The following experience will strengthen your application:***


* A strong background in software engineering, including requirements, architecture, design, coding, verification and validation.
* A strong background in applied AI or AI Engineering, including making large and small AI models work for practical problems.
* Experience in agentic AI, with multiple AI models working in coordination towards a common problem, often involving legacy tools and systems.
* A good background in conceptual modeling, including, for example, UML, goal models, or architectural models.
* Demonstrated skills in technical writing and programming.

**What you will do**
--------------------


* Take courses at an advanced level within the Graduate school of Computer Science and Engineering
* Develop your own scientific concepts and communicate the results of your research verbally and in writing.
* The position generally also includes teaching on Chalmers' undergraduate level or performing other duties corresponding to 20 percent of working hours.
* Research and develop theoretical foundations, new methods, models, and tools to observe and monitor modern and complex agentic AI software systems.
* Evaluate theories, methods, models and solutions using rigorous empirical methods, both in the lab and potentially in industrial contexts.

**Contract terms**
------------------


* The Doctoral student positions are fully funded from start.
* The position is a fixed-term appointment of four years, with the possibility to teach up to 20%, which extends the position up to five years.
* A starting salary of 34,550 SEK per month (valid from May 25, 2025).
* Doctoral studies require physical presence throughout the entire study period. A valid residence permit must be presented by the study start date; otherwise the admission may be withdrawn.

**What we offer**
-----------------


* As a Doctoral student at Chalmers, you are an employee and enjoy all employee benefits. Read more about working at Chalmers and our benefits for employees.
* A dynamic and inspiring working environment in the coastal city of Gothenburg.
* Read more about Sweden’s generous parental leave, subsidized day care, free schools, healthcare etc at Move To Gothenburg.


Chalmers is dedicated to improving gender balance and actively works with equality projects, such as the GENIE Initiative for gender equality and excellence. We celebrate diversity and consider equality and inclusion as fundamental aspects of all our activities.



If Swedish is not your native language, Chalmers offers Swedish courses to help you settle in.



Find more general information about doctoral studies at Chalmers here.


**Application procedure**
-------------------------


*The application should be written in English and attached as PDF-files, as below. Maximum size for each file is 40 MB. Please note that the system does not support Zip files.*


**CV**


**Personal letter**


* A brief introduction about yourself.
* A brief motivation as to why you are interested in this position.


**Bachelor’s and, if available, master’s thesis together with the transcripts.**


**(Optional) Any Peer-reviewed published research paper**



Use the button at the foot of the page to reach the application form.  

  

A background check may be conducted as part of the application process.  

  

**Please note:** The applicant is responsible for ensuring that the application is complete. Incomplete applications and applications sent by email will not be considered. Contact details to references will be requested after the interview.



We welcome your application no later than **June 12****th**.


#### **For questions, please contact:**

Jennifer Horkoff  

Associate Professor/Universitetslektor (Docent)  

jenho@chalmers.se/jennifer.horkoff@gu.se


### **We look forward to your application!**


*Chalmers declines to consider all offers of further announcement publishing or other types of support for the recruiting process in connection with this position.*



*Chalmers University of Technology in Gothenburg conducts research and education in technology and natural sciences at a high international level. The university has 3100 employees and 10,000 students, and offers education in engineering, science, shipping and architecture. With scientific excellence as a basis, Chalmers promotes knowledge and technical solutions for a sustainable world. Through global commitment and entrepreneurship, we foster an innovative spirit, in close collaboration with wider society.*  

*Chalmers was founded in 1829 and has the same motto today as it did then: Avancez – forward.*

title: System Engineer – Central Electronic Module (CEM)
description:
Job description
  
  

Are you passionate about automotive software systems and turning complex system needs into clear, high-quality software requirements?
  
  

We are looking for a System Engineer with strong experience in Requirements Engineering and automotive electronics to join an exciting project focused on next-generation vehicle architecture.
  
  

Gothenburg, Sweden
  
Automotive Industry
  
**Experience Level:** Mid-Senior (5–6+ years)
  
**Language:** English (Fluent)
  
  

Your Mission
  
  

As a System Engineer within Central Electronic Modules (CEM), you will play a key role in defining, analysing, and managing software requirements for critical vehicle electronic systems. You will work closely with architects, developers, testers, and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure software requirements are clear, testable, and fully traceable throughout the development lifecycle.
  
  

Your Responsibilities
  
  

Capture, analyse, and refine software and system requirements
  
Translate stakeholder needs into detailed functional and non-functional software requirements
  
Break down system requirements into function and component-level specifications
  
Ensure requirement traceability, consistency, and impact analysis across development phases
  
Support software architects and development teams with clear technical specifications
  
Collaborate with verification teams to define acceptance criteria and support test case creation
  
Drive requirement quality in both V-model and Agile development environments
  
Support change management activities and requirement updates throughout the project lifecycle
  
  

Required Skills & Experience
  
  

5+ years of experience in automotive software or system engineering
  
Strong experience working with CEM (Central Electronic Module) systems
  
Solid background in requirements engineering using tools such as IBM DOORS, SystemWeaver, or equivalent
  
Good understanding of automotive communication protocols such as CAN Bus, LIN Bus, and Automotive Ethernet
  
Experience with requirement traceability, impact analysis, and change management
  
Knowledge of verification and validation methodologies
  
Understanding of ASPICE (ISO 26262 knowledge is a plus)
  
Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Computer Engineering, Software Engineering, Mechatronics, or related field
  
  

What We Offer
  
  

An international community bringing together 110+ different nationalities
  
**An environment where trust has a central place:** 70% of key leaders started their careers at entry-level positions
  
A robust training system with an internal Academy and 250+ available modules
  
A vibrant workplace with frequent internal events (afterworks, team buildings, etc.)
  
**A strong commitment to sustainability:** the opportunity to turn ideas into action and make a tangible impact
  
Through the WeCare Together program, the possibility to design and lead projects with real social or environmental impact, with full company support
  
  

**Salary Range:** From 46,667 SEK to 56,667 SEK (gross per month)
  
  

The stated salary falls within the established salary range for the position and is determined based on experience, skills, and level of responsibility, in accordance with our policy of fair and non-discriminatory compensation.
  
  

Amaris Consulting is proud to be an equal-opportunity workplace. We are committed to promoting diversity within the workforce and creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome applications from all qualified candidates regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, ethnicity, beliefs, age, marital status, disability, or other characteristics.
  
  

Who are we?
  
Amaris Consulting is an independent technology consulting firm providing guidance and solutions to businesses. With more than 1000 clients across the globe, we have been rolling out solutions in major projects for over a decade – this is made possible by an international team of 7,600 people spread across 5 continents and more than 60 countries. Our solutions focus on four different Business Lines: Information System & Digital, Telecom, Life Sciences and Engineering. We’re focused on building and nurturing a top talent community where all our team members can achieve their full potential. Amaris is your steppingstone to cross rivers of change, meet challenges and achieve all your projects with success.
  
  

At Amaris, we strive to provide our candidates with the best possible recruitment experience. We like to get to know our candidates, challenge them, and be able to give them proper feedback as quickly as possible. Here's what our recruitment process looks like:
  
  

**Brief Call:** Our process typically begins with a brief virtual/phone conversation to get to know you! The objective? Learn about you, understand your motivations, and make sure we have the right job for you!
  
  

Interviews (the average number of interviews is 3 - the number may vary depending on the level of seniority required for the position). During the interviews, you will meet people from our team: your line manager of course, but also other people related to your future role. We will talk in depth about you, your experience, and skills, but also about the position and what will be expected of you. Of course, you will also get to know Amaris: our culture, our roots, our teams, and your career opportunities!
  
  

**Case study:** Depending on the position, we may ask you to take a test. This could be a role play, a technical assessment, a problem-solving scenario, etc.
  
  

As you know, every person is different and so is every role in a company. That is why we have to adapt accordingly, and the process may differ slightly at times. However, please know that we always put ourselves in the candidate's shoes to ensure they have the best possible experience.
  
We look forward to meeting you!

title: Start-up söker Systemutevecklare
description:
About the Job
  
About Arkeon Technologies
  
Arkeon Technologies is a spinout from Chalmers and WACQT. We are developing a room-temperature post-fabrication process that adjusts the resistance of Josephson junctions on superconducting quantum chips to stabilize and fine-tune qubit frequencies. Our technology reduces frequency variations that cause collisions, crosstalk, and low yield, making scalable quantum technology more achievable.
  
The Role – Master of Science in Engineering, Systems Developer
  
We are looking for an engineer who enjoys combining hands-on lab work, embedded systems, and technical analysis. The role suits someone with experience working closely with electronics, prototypes, sensors, or embedded systems, and who wants to develop within testing, measurement, and hardware-level troubleshooting.
  
As a systems developer, you will work with testing, measurements, and verification of Arkeon's technology. The work involves data collection, documentation, and development of algorithms, mathematical models, and/or test code in Python and Go.
  
Responsibilities
  
Perform lab tests and measurements on prototypes, electronics modules, and test equipment
  
Collect, structure, and process technical KPIs and measurement data
  
Troubleshoot hardware- and software-related issues in test setups
  
Contribute to improvements in test processes and the development of test tools
  
Document results in internal reports and technical logs
  
Develop algorithms and mathematical models
  
  

Requirements
  
Degree in mechatronics, electronics, embedded systems, control engineering, or equivalent (MSc or PhD)
  
Hands-on experience with electronics, sensors, embedded systems, or measurement equipment
  
Strong proficiency in Python and Go.
  
Experience with hardware-level troubleshooting, development, or prototype work is highly valued
  
**A plus:** experience working in a startup environment
  
  

Work Environment
  
With us, you will be a central part of a team building technical solutions from the ground up. You will have the opportunity to work closely with prototypes and hardware, directly influence product development, and see how your work drives real customer demonstrations. The role is ideal for someone who wants to grow within laboratory work, test development, measurement technology, and embedded systems, supported by senior engineers in an open and innovative environment.

title: Experienced Method Engineer
description:
Transport is at the core of modern society. Imagine using your expertise to shape sustainable transport and infrastructure solutions for the future. If you seek to make a difference on a global scale, working with next-gen technologies and the sharpest collaborative teams, then we could be a perfect match.


Volvo Construction Equipment in Eskilstuna is strengthening our methods organization to meet today’s manufacturing needs and tomorrow’s digital service and maintenance landscape. We are looking for a Method Engineer with strong machine understanding, ideally with hands-on experience from physical work on machines, combined with curiosity and capability in emerging areas such as AI, VR/AR and data analytics for predictive maintenance.  

About the role  

Our operating model is evolving, and so are the needs of competence for method engineering. The role is moving from mainly operative, mechanic-focused work toward a broader profile: mechanic/electronic/data analytics competence, agile ways of working, and a stronger focus on future business critical capability building (including AI, predictive maintenance and VR/AR). You will support both “transform and perform” by improving current deliveries while building capability for future projects.

 **What you will do**  

* Lead development and continuous improvement of manufacturing methods within your area, acting as a specialist and go-to support.
* Participate early in product introductions, changes and feasibility studies to ensure manufacturability and robust methods.
* Drive rationalizations and improvements, sharing best practices across the methods area and supporting operational excellence.
* Ensure method and process standards are met and maintained; contribute to quality development work and structured feedback loops.
* Lead or contribute to projects investigating and implementing solutions that improve safety, quality, cost and delivery performance.
* Support tooling and investment needs, including introducing and phasing out tools and monitoring tool cost development.
* Act as a trainer/coach in your methods area and help develop needed training and competence plans (2–3-year method strategy).
* Collaborate cross-functionally (production, preparation, product development, IT/data) and with global stakeholders to deliver and scale methods.
* Contribute to digitalization initiatives, e.g., identifying the right data points, enabling data collection and analysis, and supporting use cases such as predictive diagnostics and maintenance.
* Help mature ways of working from traditional approaches toward more agile, learning-oriented and digital methods.
**Future competence focus (AI, VR/AR & analytics)**

  

You will be part of a competence shift that strengthens method engineering with new digital capabilities. Depending on your background, you may contribute to one or several of the following areas:

* Predictive diagnostics and maintenance: service/production data understanding, analysis, and practical application.
* Digital service platforms and data ecosystems: service data platforms, digital twins, AI-enabled assistants, workshop/production software.
* AR/VR and simulation: supporting service readiness, training, and method verification through immersive tools.
* Cross-domain integration: working across mechanical, electrical/electronic and software domains in an integrated ecosystem.
* Service intelligence and analytics: developing insights that improve availability, maintainability and lifecycle cost.
 **Who you are**  

You combine a solid technical foundation with a pragmatic, hands-on mindset. You enjoy guiding others, making continuous improvements and balancing short-term delivery with long-term capability building.

 **Requirements**  

* University degree in engineering (or equivalent experience).
* Strong machine understanding: experience from working physically with machines (e.g., service/maintenance/assembly) is highly valued.
* Solid understanding of manufacturing capability, methods and process standards.
* Structured problem-solving skills and ability to work in complex environments.
* Good knowledge of MS Office.
* Fluent in Swedish and English.
 **Meriting**  

* Experience or strong interest in data analytics, AI and/or predictive maintenance.
* Interest or experience in VR/AR, simulation or digital development environments.
* Knowledge of CAD, simulation or other digital engineering tools.
* Experience working in agile or iterative development ways of working.
* Benchmarking experience and participation in development forums/networks.
**What’s in it for you?**
-------------------------

**Your authority**  

* Stop production to address quality deviations.
* Block incorrect material.
* Prioritize activities within your methods area.
* Make method choices within your responsibility.
 **What we offer**  

* The role where classic mechanics meets digitalization—help shape how methods evolve with AI, data and immersive tools.
* Opportunity to drive real change in an organization on a transformation journey with a strong focus on digital ways of working.
* Competence development opportunities and collaboration with international teams and experts.
* A supportive environment where you can influence, improve and standardize methods with impact on safety, quality and efficiency.
**Ready for the next move?**

  

If you are eager to make a meaningful impact and grow with us, we look forward to receiving your application.

 **Inclusion, Diversity & Equity**  

We want everyone to bring their full authentic self to work and thrive in a safe and supportive environment. Even if you don’t meet every qualification, we encourage you to apply, let’s decide together if this role is the right match.  

Volvo Construction Equipment is part of the Volvo Group, driven by our purpose to build the world we want to live in. Join us and help build a better tomorrow.

 **For questions, please contact:**  

Mia Mäkäräinen, Head of AM Engineering Methods, mia.makarainen@volvo.com  

Åsa Öberg, People & Culture Partner, asa.oberg@volvo.com  

**Last day for apply:** May 27th  

**Location:** Eskilstuna

  

We value your data privacy and therefore do not accept applications via mail.

 **Who we are and what we believe in**  

We are committed to shaping the future landscape of efficient, safe, and sustainable transport solutions. Fulfilling our mission creates countless career opportunities for talents across the group’s leading brands and entities.

  

Applying to this job offers you the opportunity to join **Volvo Group**. Every day, you will be working with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to be able to leave our society in better shape for the next generation. We are passionate about what we do, and we thrive on teamwork. We are almost 100,000 people united around the world by a culture of care, inclusiveness, and empowerment.

  

Part of Volvo Group, **Volvo Construction Equipment** is a global company driven by our purpose to build the world we want to live in. Together we develop and deliver solutions for a cleaner, smarter, and more connected world. By unleashing everyone’s full potential, we build a more sustainable future for all our stakeholders. Come join our team and help us build a better tomorrow.
company_description: Every day, we work with some of the sharpest and most creative brains in our field to leave our society in better shape for the next generation.

description:

title: Field Sales Engineer
description:
Are you a professional seeking a new challenge in a dynamic and fast developing business?  



  

As an **Field Sales Engineer** (Instrumentation), you will become part of the Nordics Global Sales team, with immediate responsibilities for Central Sweden and Norway, based in Karlstad, Sweden.


This role is essential for primarily developing, as well as maintaining and improving direct customer contacts and relationships in line with the defined "Go to Market" philosophy for **the Chemical, Pulp and Paper industries, and other land based sectors across Norway and central Sweden.** By engaging with our customers, you will work towards delivering the company's ambitious growth targets. The focus is on selling and promoting products, services, concepts, and solutions offered by the Measurement Solutions Business Unit.


Let's Go, together! We offer a dynamic environment where individual contributions and inventions are valued and provide opportunities for training and self-development.


Please note we follow the process of continuous selection and will close the vacancy as soon as we find the right candidate, so do not delay submitting the application.  



**In This Role, Your Responsibilities Will Be:**

* Promoting products and services across the assigned sales area
* Visiting key accounts and prospects to assess potential and strengthen relationships
* Understanding customer drivers and staying current with their business needs
* Preparing annual booking budgets by product, value and period
* Submitting call plans, analyses and reports with timely accuracy
* Reporting sales plans and project potential through CRM updates
* Liaising with internal teams to prioritize quotations and maintain service levels
Participating in relevant industry events  
* 

**Who You Are:**

You actively listen and check for understanding, and you adjust communication content and style to meet the needs of diverse stakeholders.


Your proactive approach to identifying and pursuing new business opportunities sets you apart. You anticipate customer needs and provide services that are beyond their expectations, always aiming to expand the customer base and drive sales growth.


You use customer insights to drive and guide the development of new offerings, fostering collaboration across the organization and ensuring that everyone works together towards common goals.  



**For This Role, You Will Need:**

* Technical degree, or equivalent experience
* Proven sales / business development experience in a commercial role
* Exposure to industrial automation environment (e.g. chemical, petrochemical, oil & gas, power, life science, refining), with a good technical understanding (ideally measuring instrumentation)
* CRM & MS Office tools
* Proficiency in English and Norwegian language
* Willingness to travel 50% of the time in the country and regionally
Driver’s license  
* 

**Preferred Qualifications that Set You Apart:**

* Previous experience with flow measurement solutions
* Matrix organization experience
Interest in energy efficiency and sustainability  
* 

**Our Culture & Commitment to You**

At Emerson, we prioritize a workplace where every employee is valued, respected, and empowered to grow. We foster an environment that encourages innovation, collaboration, and diverse perspectives—because we know that great ideas come from great teams.


Our commitment to ongoing career development and growing an inclusive culture ensures you have the support to thrive. Whether through mentorship, training, or leadership opportunities, we invest in your success so you can make a lasting impact. We believe diverse teams, working together are key to driving growth and delivering business results.  



**What We Offer**

* A varied and creative role in the exciting and fast-growing field of automation
* Excellent opportunities for personal growth and long-term career development within EMERSON, a global leader
* A supportive and welcoming atmosphere within our dynamic Nordic team
* An international environment where motivated people truly make an impact
* Flexible working hours and the option to partially work from home
* A neutral company car available for both business and private use
* An attractive sales incentive plan (SIP) / bonus program
* Collective Agreement
* Pension plan
* AFP
* Accident and life insurance
* Other (computer glasses, wellness allowance, subsidized lunch, optional insurances and many more)

  
**WHY EMERSON**

**Our Commitment to Our People**

At Emerson, we are motivated by a spirit of collaboration that helps our diverse, multicultural teams across the world drive innovation that makes the world healthier, safer, smarter, and more sustainable. And we want you to join us in our bold aspiration.


We have built an engaged community of inquisitive, dedicated people who thrive knowing they are welcomed, trusted, celebrated, and empowered to solve the world’s most complex problems — for our customers, our communities, and the planet. You’ll contribute to this vital work while further developing your skills through our award-winning employee development programs. We are a proud corporate citizen in every city where we operate and are committed to our people, our communities, and the world at large. We take this responsibility seriously and strive to make a positive impact through every endeavor.


At Emerson, you’ll see firsthand that our people are at the center of everything we do. So, let’s go. Let’s think differently. Learn, collaborate, and grow. Seek opportunity. Push boundaries. Be empowered to make things better. Speed up to break through. Let’s go, together.


**Accessibility Assistance or Accommodation**

If you have a disability and are having difficulty accessing or using this website to apply for a position, please contact: idisability.administrator@emerson.com .  



**ABOUT EMERSON**

Emerson is a global leader in automation technology and software. Through our deep domain expertise and legacy of flawless execution, Emerson helps customers in critical industries like life sciences, energy, power and renewables, chemical and advanced factory automation operate more sustainably while improving productivity, energy security and reliability.


With global operations and a comprehensive portfolio of software and technology, we are helping companies implement digital transformation to measurably improve their operations, conserve valuable resources and enhance their safety.


We offer equitable opportunities, celebrate diversity, and embrace challenges with confidence that, together, we can make an impact across a broad spectrum of countries and industries. Whether you’re an established professional looking for a career change, an undergraduate student exploring possibilities, or a recent graduate with an advanced degree, you’ll find your chance to make a difference with Emerson. Join our team – let’s go!


**No calls or agencies please.**

title: Vill du jobba med ILS inom avancerad försvarsteknik?
description:
**Vill du jobba med ILS inom avancerad försvarsteknik?**


Vill du arbeta med framtidssäkra lösningar för nationell och internationell säkerhet? Affärsområde Strategic Product Solutions är i stark tillväxt och söker nu flera ILS‑ingenjörer till några av våra mest spännande och strategiskt viktiga uppdrag.


Sylog är ett konsultbolag med starkt fokus på våra medarbetare och bidrar med vår kompetens till organisationer som arbetar med avancerad produkt-, tjänste- och systemutveckling. Hos oss får du som konsult möjlighet att växa och utvecklas – på dina egna villkor.

**Det här erbjuder vi**


Hos Sylog får du en trygg anställning och stora möjligheter till personlig och professionell utveckling. Genom interna kunskapsutbyten, kurser och inspirerande seminarier kan du själv välja hur du vill stärka och bredda din kompetens. Du får också möjlighet att utvecklas inom ILS (Integrated Lifecycle Support) samt närliggande områden som t ex systemsäkerhet och systems engineering.


Du blir en del av spännande projekt där vi levererar expertis som förvandlar idéer till verklighet. Med fokus på både teknik och människor är vi den konsultpartner som stöttar våra kunder med kvalitativa leveranser inom flyg, marin, ledning, mark och rymd.


Hos oss möts du av ett engagerat och positivt team präglat av kamratskap, öppenhet, hjälpsamhet och hög kompetens. Vi värdesätter gemenskap och har många aktiviteter där vi umgås och har roligt tillsammans.


Vi arbetar med både nationella och internationella projekt och anställer kompetens inom hela Sverige.

**Din roll som ILS-konsult**


Som ILS-ingenjör arbetar du i våra in-house team eller direkt mot kund inom teknikområdet ILS. Du har en mycket viktig roll för att möjliggöra tillgänglig, kostnadseffektiv och säker lösning för de system och produkter vi arbetar med. Genom olika typer av systemtekniska analyser, kommer du vara med och påverka kravhantering, design, supportlösning och resursförsörjning. Du kommer bl a att arbeta med att optimera tillgänglighet, effektivitet och reducerad livscykelkostnad. Syftet är att säkerställa att våra kunders kritiskt viktiga produkter och system fungerar när de behövs som bäst. Du har möjlighet att arbeta inom hela ILS-området, såsom ledning och strategi, underhållsplanering, reservdelsoptimering, tillförlitlighet, livscykelanalys, teknisk information samt training. Du kan också få arbeta med uppgifter inom bl.a. systemsäkerhet eller systemutveckling.


Du kommer ingå i ett härligt team med olika erfarenhetsnivå och kompetensbakgrund – vi tror att detta skapar bättre problemlösning och kreativitet, viktig kunskapsöverföring, högre kvalitet samt ökad trivsel och teamkänsla. Resor i tjänsten kan förekomma.

**Kvalifikationer**


Vi söker dig som är teknikintresserad och vill jobba med människor. Du trivs i en miljö där nära samarbete med kunder och kollegor är en naturlig del av vardagen. För oss är det en självklarhet att dela med oss av kunskap och erfarenheter för att lösa våra åtaganden tillsammans och för att ständigt utveckla vår kompetens och bygga ny erfarenhet.


Vi tror att du som söker är civil- eller högskoleingenjör inom något teknikområde, eller har annan relevant bakgrund från till exempel försvar och försvarsindustri. Har du några års erfarenhet eller tidigare erfarenheter inom kompetensområdet är det meriterande. Är du ny inom ILS-området kommer du omsorgsfullt bli introducerad av erfarna kollegor samt successivt genomgå relevanta utbildningar kring metodik och processer.


Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska är ett krav för tjänsten. Då flera av våra tjänster innebär arbete som omfattas av försvarssekretess, krävs det att du genomför och godkänns i en säkerhetsprövning.

***Web*** ***|*** ***Linkedin*** ***|*** ***Instagram***

*Sylog is a consulting group specialized in Software Development and Digitalization. Our customers are world leading companies in telecom, automotive, defense, life science, finance, media and gaming. Our Swedish offices are located in Stockholm, Uppsala, Linköping, Göteborg, Malmö, Växjö, Östersund and Skövde. As a part of Akkodis we are represented in 30 countries by more than 50.000 employees.*
